description: Perena builds unified stablecoin infrastructure on Solana, centered on Numéraire, a hub-and-spoke stableswap AMM that connects USDC, USDT, PYUSD and others through a shared liquidity hub. Its USD* token is a liquid, yield-integrated savings asset. Numéraire became the largest stableswap on Solana, providing a single access point for projects to launch and integrate stablecoins. Founded by ex-Solana stablecoin lead Anna Yuan.
notable: Hub-and-spoke stableswap; largest stableswap on Solana.

name: Persona
tagline: Configurable identity verification platform
description: Persona is a configurable identity verification and KYC/KYB platform with orchestration, document and biometric verification, watchlist screening and case management, widely used by crypto, fintech and on/off-ramp companies for onboarding compliance.
notable: Highly configurable identity orchestration for crypto onboarding.

name: Phantom
tagline: Leading multichain wallet, dominant on Solana
description: Phantom is a consumer self-custody wallet with ~17M monthly active users and roughly 39% of the Solana wallet market. It supports Solana, Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Bitcoin and Sui, with USDC and USDT support across Solana and EVM chains plus deep Solana DeFi integrations (Jupiter, Kamino, MarginFi).
notable: The default wallet of the Solana ecosystem.

name: Phemex
tagline: User-first derivatives and spot crypto exchange
description: Phemex is a crypto exchange founded in 2019 by former Morgan Stanley executives, serving 10M+ users. It offers spot and derivatives trading with up to 100x leverage across 500+ perpetual futures (USDT-M, USDC-M, Coin-M), options, and a 100% proof-of-reserves system.
notable: Founded by ex-Morgan Stanley team; offers crypto options trading.

name: PhonePe
tagline: India's leading UPI payments app
description: PhonePe is India's largest UPI-based digital payments platform, processing the majority of the country's Unified Payments Interface transactions and offering merchant payments, bill pay, insurance, and lending. It has expanded into crypto and digital-asset adjacencies and is positioned at the intersection of mass-market payments and on/off-ramp rails as India's digital-money ecosystem evolves.
notable: Processes the largest share of India's UPI transaction volume.

name: Picasso (Composable)
tagline: IBC interoperability beyond Cosmos
description: Picasso, built by the Composable Foundation, extends the Cosmos IBC protocol to chains outside Cosmos, becoming the first to connect Ethereum to Cosmos via IBC and later extending to Solana and Polkadot/Kusama. It provides trust-minimized cross-chain transfer of assets and data, including stablecoins, with security stronger than conventional bridges.
notable: First to bring Cosmos IBC to Ethereum.

name: Pintu
tagline: Mobile-first Indonesian crypto app and wallet
description: Pintu is a mobile-first Indonesian crypto trading and investing app offering 320+ assets, futures and a built-in custodial wallet to store, send and receive crypto. Aimed at beginners, it is registered with and supervised by Indonesian regulators (OJK/Bappebti).
notable: Among the first crypto platforms supervised by Indonesia's financial regulator.

name: Plasma
tagline: Stablecoin-first L1 for zero-fee USDT transfers
description: Plasma is a Bitcoin-anchored, EVM-compatible Layer-1 purpose-built around USDT, backed by Tether and Bitfinex. It uses PlasmaBFT consensus, anchors state checkpoints to Bitcoin, and offers zero-fee USDT transfers, sub-second finality, and gas payable in stablecoins. Its XPL token launched with mainnet in late 2025, and it grew rapidly in stablecoin TVL into 2026.
funding: Backed by Tether/Bitfinex; raised via ICO and venture rounds
notable: Tether/Bitfinex-aligned; positioned as a direct challenger to TRON for USDT settlement.

name: Plume Network
tagline: Modular L1/L2 blockchain for RWAs
description: Plume is an EVM-compatible blockchain purpose-built for real-world assets, with KYC/AML and compliance baked in at the protocol level. It hosts tokenized treasuries, private credit, real estate, gold (via Matrixdock) and commodities, launching mainnet with $150M+ in RWAs deployed.
funding: $30M (Haun Ventures, Galaxy, Brevan Howard); PLUME token
notable: Dedicated RWA chain with compliance at the protocol level.

name: Plutus
tagline: Crypto rewards Visa debit card
description: Plutus is a UK fintech offering a Visa debit card with up to 3% cashback paid in PLU tokens, letting users spend crypto and stablecoins across Europe with tiered rewards.
notable: Long-running European crypto rewards debit card.

name: PNC Bank
tagline: Large US regional bank entering crypto services
description: PNC Bank is a large US bank that partnered with Coinbase to offer crypto custody and trading services to its clients, positioning it to support stablecoin reserves, banking, and institutional digital-asset custody.
notable: Partnered with Coinbase in 2025 to bring crypto custody and trading to its clients.

name: Polygon
tagline: Low-cost EVM network for stablecoin payments at scale
description: Polygon (PoS and the AggLayer ecosystem) is a low-cost EVM-compatible network widely used for stablecoin payments, remittances, and consumer fintech integrations. It hosts substantial USDC and USDT liquidity and is a supported settlement network for payment processors like Mastercard and Stripe.
notable: Widely integrated by PSPs for low-cost stablecoin settlement.

name: Polymer Labs
tagline: IBC interoperability hub for Ethereum rollups
description: Polymer Labs builds an interoperability hub bringing IBC to Ethereum and its rollups, enabling trust-minimized cross-chain messaging via proofs and a Prove API. It positions itself as the interop layer for Ethereum L2s and stablecoin movement between them.
notable: Brings IBC-style interoperability to Ethereum rollups.

name: Pomelo
tagline: Card issuing and payments infrastructure for Latin America
description: Pomelo is an API-first card issuing, processing and payments infrastructure platform for Latin America, serving 150+ banks, fintechs and corporates including Santander, BBVA, Western Union, Rappi and DolarApp. Founded in 2021, it raised a $55M Series C and is building a stablecoin-native global card and payment tokenization.
notable: Building a stablecoin-native global card for LatAm fintechs.

name: Portal Bridge
tagline: Token bridge powered by Wormhole
description: Portal Bridge is the flagship token bridge built on Wormhole that lets users move assets and stablecoins across 30+ chains, including Solana, Ethereum, Sui, and Aptos. It supports both wrapped-asset transfers and native CCTP USDC routes.
notable: Primary Wormhole consumer bridge across 30+ chains.

name: Pragma
tagline: Verifiable oracle for Starknet and beyond
description: Pragma is a decentralized, verifiable oracle native to Starknet, providing transparent first-party price feeds and randomness with on-chain computation. It is expanding beyond the Starknet ecosystem to other chains, targeting DeFi and derivatives protocols that need composable, auditable data.
notable: Leading native oracle of the Starknet ecosystem.

name: Predicate
tagline: Programmable policy for blockchain applications
description: Predicate is a policy layer that lets applications define and enforce transaction rules, enabling on-chain compliance, KYC/AML screening, and conditional execution for financial products including stablecoins and DeFi protocols.
notable: Provides programmable, off-chain-verified policy enforcement for on-chain transactions.

name: Prisma Finance
tagline: LST-backed stablecoin mkUSD
description: Prisma Finance is a Liquity-fork CDP protocol that lets users mint mkUSD against liquid staking tokens such as wstETH, cbETH, rETH and sfrxETH. It launched on Ethereum mainnet in 2023. The protocol suffered a significant exploit in 2024.
notable: Prominent LSDFi CDP stablecoin; hit by a 2024 exploit.

name: Progmat
tagline: Japan's bank-grade digital asset and stablecoin issuance platform
description: Progmat, Inc. is a Tokyo-based digital asset infrastructure company founded by MUFG and backed by SMBC, Mizuho, JPX, SBI and others. Its Progmat Coin platform provides compliant, multi-chain issuance and management infrastructure for yen- and dollar-backed stablecoins, with MUFG Trust Bank acting as custodian to ensure 1:1 backing. Progmat underpins Japan's megabank stablecoin initiatives and supports issuance partners including Binance Japan.
funding: Backed by MUFG, SMBC, Mizuho, JPX, SBI
notable: National-scale digital asset platform underpinning Japan's bank-issued yen stablecoins.

name: Propine
tagline: Asia's first CMS-licensed institutional digital-asset custodian
description: Propine is a Singapore-based institutional digital-asset custodian, the first in Asia to hold a Capital Markets Services (CMS) custody license from the Monetary Authority of Singapore after graduating from the MAS regulatory sandbox in 2021. It offers audited, insured custody and tokenization of real-world assets. Nomura-backed custodian Komainu agreed to acquire Propine in 2024.
notable: First MAS CMS-licensed digital-asset custodian in Asia; being acquired by Nomura-backed Komainu.

name: Provenance Blockchain (Figure)
tagline: Blockchain for tokenized private credit at scale
description: Provenance Blockchain, originated by Figure Technologies, is a public blockchain underpinning tens of billions in tokenized loans, HELOCs and private-credit assets. Figure is one of the largest originators of on-chain consumer credit and tokenized real-world debt.
notable: Among the largest RWA networks by tokenized loan volume.

name: Pyth Network
tagline: Low-latency first-party financial oracle
description: Pyth Network is a financial oracle delivering 400+ low-latency, high-fidelity price feeds across crypto, FX, equities, ETFs, and commodities. It uses a pull model where 95+ first-party publishers (exchanges and market makers) post signed prices to Pythnet, and applications across 90+ chains pull updates on demand. Widely used for stablecoin pricing and perps.
notable: Pull-oracle pioneer with 90+ chain coverage and first-party publisher model.

name: QiDao / Mai Finance
tagline: Multi-chain overcollateralized stablecoin MAI
description: QiDao Protocol (Mai Finance) issues MAI (miMATIC), an overcollateralized stablecoin minted against a wide range of crypto collateral via CDP vaults across many EVM chains. QI is the governance token.
notable: Among the most multi-chain CDP stablecoins.

name: Qivalis
tagline: MiCA-era euro stablecoin backed by a European banking consortium
description: qivalis is an Amsterdam-based company formed by a consortium of major European banks to issue a MiCA-compliant euro-pegged stablecoin, targeting a launch in the second half of 2026. The shareholder banks include ING, UniCredit, BNP Paribas, Raiffeisen Bank International, SEB, Danske Bank, CaixaBank, KBC, Banca Sella and DekaBank. It is led by Jan-Oliver Sell, a former CEO of Coinbase's German operation, with ING's Floris Lugt as CFO and former NatWest chair Howard Davies heading the board.
notable: Backed by a consortium of European banks; aims to launch a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin before the ECB's digital euro.

name: Qredo
tagline: Decentralized MPC custody network
description: Qredo is a decentralized custody network using MPC to distribute key shares across nodes, providing self-custody with off-chain settlement for institutions. It pioneered a decentralized approach to institutional key management, though it has restructured significantly amid market headwinds.
notable: Pioneer of decentralized MPC custody.

name: Quantoz Payments
tagline: Issuer of EURQ and USDQ
description: Quantoz Payments is a Dutch electronic money institution supervised by De Nederlandsche Bank that received MiCA authorization for EURQ (euro) and USDQ (US dollar) stablecoins, backed by major partners including Tether, Fabric Ventures and Kraken.
funding: Backed by Tether, Kraken, Fabric Ventures
notable: MiCA-authorized Dutch issuer backed by Tether and Kraken.

name: Quantstamp
tagline: Smart contract auditing and Web3 security
description: Quantstamp is a blockchain security firm providing smart contract audits, formal verification, and risk assessments for DeFi protocols, stablecoins, and tokenization projects. It has audited a large number of Ethereum-based contracts securing billions in value.
notable: One of the earliest dedicated smart contract auditing firms.

name: Quidax
tagline: Nigeria's first SEC-licensed crypto exchange
description: Quidax is a Nigerian cryptocurrency exchange that enables users to buy, sell and store crypto and stablecoins with local currency, and provides a B2B API for businesses to add crypto features. In September 2024 it became the first crypto exchange to receive a provisional operating license from Nigeria's SEC, and it serves customers across 70+ countries.
notable: First crypto exchange to receive a provisional SEC license in Nigeria.

name: R25
tagline: Institutional-grade yield-bearing RWA stablecoin protocol
description: R25 is an RWA and stablecoin protocol bringing institutional-grade real-world assets onchain. Its flagship rcUSD/rcUSD+ is a yield-bearing tokenized dollar backed by short-duration US Treasury bills and money-market instruments, designed to hold a 1:1 dollar peg while paying onchain yield. It launched on Polygon as its first EVM partner and has expanded to Sui with rcUSD and rcUSDp.
notable: Yield-bearing RWA stablecoin protocol launched on Polygon and Sui.

name: Rabby Wallet
tagline: The EVM power-user wallet with transaction simulation
description: Rabby is an open-source EVM wallet built by the DeBank team supporting 140+ EVM chains. Its standout feature is best-in-class pre-transaction simulation, showing expected balance changes, contract reputation, and approval scope before signing, making it a favorite for DeFi power users.
notable: Best-in-class transaction preview/simulation in the EVM category.

name: Radiant Capital
tagline: Omnichain cross-chain lending and borrowing protocol
description: Radiant Capital was an omnichain money market built on LayerZero that let users deposit on one chain and borrow on another, with stablecoins like USDC and USDT as primary assets. After an October 2024 exploit drained roughly $50M (later linked to North Korean threat actors), the DAO failed to recover and announced in June 2026 that it is winding down into a maintenance state — borrow caps zeroed, RDNT emissions ended, contracts left immutable for withdrawals.
notable: Winding down in 2026 after a ~$50M North Korea-linked exploit; protocol in maintenance mode.

name: Radom
tagline: Stablecoin payments and treasury infrastructure
description: Radom is a Web3 payments infrastructure provider offering stablecoin checkout, subscriptions, mass payouts, on/off-ramps and treasury tools via a single API, targeting businesses that want to accept and manage stablecoins globally.
notable: API-first stablecoin payments and treasury platform.

name: Raft
tagline: LST-backed stablecoin R
description: Raft is an immutable, decentralized lending protocol that allows users to mint R, an overcollateralized stablecoin, by depositing stETH or wstETH. It was a notable LSDFi protocol of 2023; the protocol experienced an exploit in late 2023.
notable: LST-backed stablecoin; suffered a 2023 exploit.

name: Rain
tagline: Stablecoin card issuing platform
description: Rain is a Visa and Mastercard principal-member card issuer that lets fintechs, exchanges, wallets and Web3 platforms launch card programs settled in stablecoins in near real time, without relying on sponsor banks. It scaled sharply through 2025-2026 and was valued at roughly $1.95B.
notable: Visa/Mastercard principal member settling card spend directly in stablecoins.

name: Rainbow
tagline: Design-forward Ethereum and EVM wallet
description: Rainbow is a consumer-friendly self-custody wallet focused on great design and UX across Ethereum and L2s (Base, Optimism, Polygon, Zora, Arbitrum). It supports token send, swaps, and dApp access, and is available in Ledger Live.
notable: Known for polished consumer-grade design.

name: RAKBANK
tagline: UAE bank approved to issue a dirham-backed stablecoin
description: RAKBANK (The National Bank of Ras Al Khaimah) is a UAE retail and commercial bank that received in-principle approval from the Central Bank of the UAE to issue a regulated AED-backed stablecoin. The planned token will be fully backed 1:1 by dirham reserves held in segregated, regulated accounts with guaranteed redemption, positioning RAKBANK among the banks driving the UAE's stablecoin ecosystem.
notable: Received CBUAE in-principle approval to issue a dirham-backed stablecoin.

name: Ramp Network
tagline: Non-custodial fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp
description: Ramp Network offers an embeddable on/off-ramp supporting 100+ assets with local rails including cards, bank transfers, Pix (Brazil), and SPEI (Mexico), plus fast document-light KYC in eligible markets. It is integrated into many leading wallets and dApps.
notable: Early non-custodial direct-to-wallet ramp pioneer.

name: Ramses / Pharaoh
tagline: Concentrated-liquidity ve(3,3) DEXs on Arbitrum and Avalanche
description: Ramses Exchange is a ve(3,3) concentrated-liquidity DEX on Arbitrum, with Pharaoh Exchange as its Avalanche-native sister deployment. Both serve as central liquidity layers for stablecoins and ecosystem tokens, offering vote-escrow tokenomics, full fee redistribution to lockers, and deep stable pools (incl. Frax and other stablecoin partners).
notable: ve(3,3) concentrated-liquidity model; key stable-liquidity hub for Frax and partners.

name: Range
tagline: Cross-chain security, monitoring and compliance
description: Range is a cross-chain security and risk platform providing real-time monitoring, transaction risk screening and compliance tooling for bridges, validators, interop protocols and stablecoin flows across chains.
notable: Cross-chain/interop-focused risk and compliance.

name: Rango Exchange
tagline: Cross-chain DEX and bridge aggregator
description: Rango Exchange is a cross-chain DEX and bridge aggregator that routes swaps and transfers across 70+ blockchains and dozens of bridges and DEXs, including non-EVM chains like Solana, Cosmos, and Tron, supporting wide stablecoin coverage.
notable: Broad multi-chain (incl. non-EVM) aggregation coverage.

name: Rapyd
tagline: Global payments network with stablecoin payouts
description: Rapyd is a global payments and fintech-as-a-service platform. In September 2025 it launched Stablecoin Payment Solutions, enabling businesses to accept, send and settle stablecoin payments instantly worldwide, layered on top of its extensive local payment-methods network across 100+ countries.
funding: $15B+ valuation; backed by Tiger Global, General Catalyst
notable: Large global payments network adding stablecoin settlement.

name: Raydium
tagline: Leading Solana AMM
description: Raydium is a top automated market maker and liquidity provider on Solana, handling large stablecoin (USDC, USDT, PYUSD, USDG) swap volume. As one of Solana's primary DEXs it underpins stablecoin liquidity on the high-throughput chain.
notable: Major Solana stablecoin trading venue.

name: RD Technologies
tagline: Issuer of HKDR, a Hong Kong dollar stablecoin
description: RD Technologies issues HKDR, a stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the Hong Kong dollar, aiming to provide a regulated digital HKD for fast, low-cost payments under Hong Kong's new stablecoin licensing regime.
notable: Among the first HKD-pegged stablecoins in Hong Kong.

name: Re7 Capital
tagline: Institutional DeFi investment firm and onchain vault curator
description: Re7 Capital is a London-based DeFi-focused investment firm managing roughly $800M, providing DeFi liquidity since 2019. Its innovation arm, Re7 Labs, is a leading onchain risk curator, managing over 100 pools across 14 chains and curating vaults on Morpho, Euler, Silo, Mellow and Kamino, ranking among the top DeFi curators by TVL.
notable: Top-five DeFi vault curator with 100+ pools across 14 chains.

name: Ready (Argent)
tagline: Starknet smart wallet with debit card
description: Ready (formerly Argent) is a self-custodial smart-wallet app on Starknet that offers a non-custodial debit card, letting users spend stablecoins on-chain while retaining custody.
notable: Argent rebranded to Ready; self-custodial Starknet spending card.

name: RealT
tagline: Fractional, tokenized US rental real estate
description: RealT is a tokenized real estate platform that fractionalizes US residential rental properties into tokens issued on Ethereum and Gnosis Chain, with token holders earning rental income distributions. Investors can buy in from as little as $50. The company has tokenized hundreds of properties, concentrated in Detroit, and has drawn legal scrutiny over property maintenance and code violations.
notable: One of the largest tokenized residential real estate operators; sold ~$94M of tokens across ~700 Detroit properties.

name: Reap
tagline: Stablecoin corporate cards and payments
description: Reap is a stablecoin financial infrastructure company that operates as a full-stack issuer of record — issuing directly and managing settlement to capture interchange, FX spread and reserve yield. It provides stablecoin-linked card issuance, spend and money-movement orchestration for businesses across Asia and beyond.
notable: Leading Asia-based stablecoin corporate card issuer.

name: RedotPay
tagline: Stablecoin card and payments for 100+ countries
description: RedotPay is one of the fastest-growing stablecoin payments networks, processing over $10B annualized as of late 2025 with 5M+ users across 100+ countries. Its card supports Apple Pay and Google Pay with built-in on/off-ramps for everyday stablecoin spending.
notable: $10B+ annualized stablecoin card volume; 5M+ users.

name: RedStone
tagline: Modular oracle for LSTs, RWAs and yield-bearing assets
description: RedStone is a fast-growing modular oracle providing on-demand and push price feeds across 110+ chains, specializing in LSTs/LRTs, RWAs, and yield-bearing stablecoins. It offers Proof of Reserve feeds (e.g. Ethena's USDtb, Lombard LBTC) and scaled to over $9B Total Value Secured by 2025. Over 170 teams use RedStone as their oracle provider.
notable: Leading oracle for yield-bearing stablecoin and RWA collateral feeds.

name: Relay
tagline: Instant low-cost cross-chain bridging and payments
description: Relay is a cross-chain bridging and execution protocol built by Reservoir that uses relayers to deliver near-instant, very low-cost transfers and cross-chain actions. It is widely embedded in wallets and apps for fast stablecoin and token movement.
notable: Very low-cost, instant cross-chain transfers; embedded in many apps.

name: Reown (WalletConnect)
tagline: WalletConnect protocol and AppKit / WalletKit
description: Reown (formerly WalletConnect Inc.) builds the WalletConnect protocol connecting 500M+ wallet connections, plus AppKit and WalletKit toolkits for embedded and connected wallet UX. WalletConnect Pay extends stablecoin payments across the WalletConnect network, including a partnership with Polygon.
notable: Operates the ubiquitous WalletConnect standard; WalletConnect Pay for stablecoins.

name: Request Finance
tagline: Crypto invoicing, payroll and stablecoin payouts
description: Request Finance is a crypto financial operations platform for invoicing, bill pay, payroll and expenses, enabling businesses and DAOs to send and receive stablecoin payments at scale with accounting and compliance tooling, built on the Request Network.
notable: Leading stablecoin invoicing and payroll for crypto businesses/DAOs.

name: Reserve Protocol
tagline: Asset-backed stablecoin baskets
description: Reserve Protocol is a permissionless platform that lets anyone create overcollateralized, asset-backed stablecoins called RTokens. Its flagship RToken, eUSD, is backed by a diversified basket of collateral and is overcollateralized via the RSR governance/staking token. Reserve also operates DTFs (Decentralized Token Folios).
funding: Raised from Coinbase Ventures, Peter Thiel, Sam Altman
notable: Permissionless asset-backed stablecoin baskets.

name: Reservoir
tagline: Issuer of rUSD yield-bearing stablecoin
description: Reservoir Protocol issues rUSD, a decentralized, scalable yield-bearing stablecoin backed by a diversified mix of RWAs and crypto collateral, with srUSD as the savings/yield-bearing version. Positioned as a next-generation efficient stablecoin protocol.
notable: Diversified-collateral decentralized yield stablecoin.

name: Resolv
tagline: Delta-neutral stablecoin protocol
description: Resolv issues USR, a delta-neutral stablecoin backed by ETH and BTC with hedged positions, and RLP, a risk-tranche insurance layer that absorbs volatility. Its yield-bearing stUSR is widely integrated into DeFi (Pendle, Morpho, Curve), making Resolv a notable newer yield-stablecoin and DeFi protocol.
notable: Tranche-based delta-neutral stablecoin with insurance layer.

name: Revolut
tagline: Global neobank with built-in crypto and stablecoin rails
description: Revolut is a global fintech super-app serving 65M+ users with banking, cards, FX, trading and crypto. It offers zero-fee 1:1 stablecoin swaps for USDC and USDT, enables USDC/USDT/POL remittances via Polygon across the UK and EEA, and plans a 2027 US bank launch with stablecoin services built in from day one.
notable: Cumulatively sent over $10.5B in stablecoin payments by end-2025, up 156% year over year.

name: Rhino.fi
tagline: Multi-chain bridge and DeFi aggregator
description: Rhino.fi is a multi-chain bridge and DeFi aggregator that enables fast, low-fee token and stablecoin transfers across 30+ chains from a single interface. It targets retail users with a self-custodial cross-chain swap and yield experience.
funding: Backed by ConsenSys, Bitfinex; DVF token
notable: Formerly DeversiFi; consumer multi-chain bridge.

name: Ripio
tagline: Latin American crypto exchange and B2B infrastructure
description: Founded in 2013, Ripio is one of Latin America's largest crypto platforms, serving millions of users across Argentina, Brazil, Colombia and other markets with trading, wallet and on/off-ramp services. It issues UXD, a USD-pegged stablecoin marketed as the first Latin American dollar stablecoin, and is building a suite of fiat-backed local-currency stablecoins to embed regional currencies on-chain.
funding: Backed by Digital Currency Group, Boost VC
notable: Issues local-currency stablecoins across Latin America and powers B2B crypto rails.

name: Ripple
tagline: Issuer of RLUSD
description: Ripple issues RLUSD, a USD stablecoin launched in December 2024 under the NYDFS framework and dual-issued on the XRP Ledger and Ethereum. RLUSD is backed by cash and US Treasuries and is integrated into Ripple's enterprise payments and custody stack.
notable: NYDFS-regulated stablecoin dual-issued on XRPL and Ethereum.

name: Rise
tagline: Global payroll for contractors and employees in fiat, stablecoins or crypto
description: Rise (Riseworks) is a global workforce-payments platform that hires, manages and pays contractors and employees across 190+ countries in local currency, stablecoins (USDC/USDT) or other crypto. Operating as a licensed money transmitter, Rise has processed over $1.3B in payroll volume, with more than half of worker withdrawals taken in stablecoins.
notable: $1.3B+ payroll processed; majority of worker withdrawals taken in stablecoins.

name: Robinhood
tagline: Retail brokerage, crypto venue and USDG co-founder
description: Robinhood is a US retail brokerage and crypto trading platform and a co-founder of the Global Dollar Network behind USDG. It offers crypto trading, tokenized stocks, and stablecoin rewards, and acquired Bitstamp to expand globally.
notable: Co-founding member of Global Dollar Network / USDG.

name: Router Protocol
tagline: Cross-chain interoperability with its own chain
description: Router Protocol is an interoperability layer built around the Router Chain, a CosmosSDK-based Layer 1 that orchestrates cross-chain messaging and asset transfers. Its Nitro bridge and Voyager front-end enable cross-chain stablecoin transfers across EVM and non-EVM chains.
notable: Operates its own Cosmos-based Router Chain for interoperability.

name: Rupiah Token
tagline: Issuer of IDRT, the Indonesian Rupiah stablecoin
description: PT Rupiah Token Indonesia issues IDRT, the first stablecoin backed by the Indonesian Rupiah. Each IDRT is backed 1:1 by Rupiah bank deposits and targets payments, remittances and local-currency trading. IDRT is issued on Ethereum and BNB Chain, with smart contracts audited by CertiK.
notable: First Indonesian Rupiah-backed stablecoin.

name: Sablier
tagline: Onchain token streaming and distribution infrastructure
description: Sablier is the first token-streaming protocol, live on Ethereum since 2019. It streams ERC-20 payments by the second and supports linear, exponential, stepped and custom distribution curves, widely used for vesting, payroll, airdrops and DAO grant distribution.
notable: The original token-streaming protocol, processing hundreds of thousands of streams.

name: Safe
tagline: The leading smart-account multisig wallet
description: Safe (formerly Gnosis Safe) is the dominant smart-contract / multisig wallet for teams, DAOs and institutions, securing tens of billions in assets. It provides programmable, multi-signature self-custody and is widely used for treasuries holding stablecoins.
notable: Secures the majority of on-chain DAO and treasury assets.

name: Safeheron
tagline: Open-source MPC + TEE self-custody
description: Safeheron provides institutional-grade self-custody combining MPC and TEE so private-key shards are never fully reconstructed. It offers white-label API integration for exchanges, OTC desks and payment providers, serving 250+ institutions with $250B+ in transfers processed.
notable: Open-source MPC + TEE collaborative custody.

name: Sardine
tagline: AI fraud, compliance and AML platform
description: Sardine is an AI risk platform combining device intelligence, behavioral biometrics and data enrichment to power fraud prevention, AML transaction monitoring, sanctions screening and case management. It is widely used by fintechs, crypto firms and stablecoin/on-ramp companies for real-time risk decisioning.
notable: Behavioral biometrics + device intelligence for fintech/crypto risk.

name: Save
tagline: Solana lending protocol, formerly Solend
description: Save (formerly Solend) is a leading algorithmic, decentralized lending and borrowing protocol on Solana. It rebranded from Solend to Save in 2024. Users supply and borrow assets including USDC and USDT across permissionless and isolated pools, with the protocol governed by its native token.
notable: Formerly Solend; one of the original and largest Solana money markets.

name: SBI Holdings
tagline: Japanese financial group bringing USDC and a yen stablecoin to Japan
description: SBI Holdings is a major Japanese financial services group that partnered with Circle to bring USDC to Japan, with its subsidiary SBI VC Trade becoming the first regulated venue to offer USDC to the Japanese public in March 2025. SBI and SBI Shinsei Bank invested $50 million in Circle, and SBI is co-developing JPYSC, a trust-bank-backed yen stablecoin, with Startale Group.
notable: SBI VC Trade was the first to offer USDC to the public in Japan.

name: Scallop
tagline: Institutional-grade lending and borrowing protocol on Sui
description: Scallop is a money-market protocol on the Sui blockchain offering lending and borrowing with a Compound/Solend-inspired design. Depositors receive interest-bearing sCoins (e.g. sUSDC) representing their position, and the protocol features soft liquidation. It is a major source of stablecoin liquidity and yield on Sui rather than a stablecoin issuer itself; governance runs through the SCA / veSCA token.
notable: One of the largest DeFi lending protocols on Sui.

name: Schuman Financial
tagline: Issuer of EURØP, a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin
description: Schuman Financial is the issuer of EURØP, a regulated, fully-backed euro stablecoin and one of the first MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins. Founded by former Binance Europe executives, it became among the first companies to acquire a stablecoin issuer (e-money token) license from France's ACPR under MiCA. EURØP is backed 1:1 by euros held in EU banks including Société Générale and is live on Ethereum, Polygon and the XRP Ledger.
notable: First MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin integrated natively on the XRP Ledger.

name: Scorechain
tagline: Crypto AML and blockchain analytics
description: Scorechain is a Luxembourg-based blockchain analytics provider offering real-time transaction monitoring, wallet screening and risk scoring across 100+ blockchains, plus travel-rule integration, for VASPs, banks and financial institutions.
notable: European AML analytics covering 100+ chains.

name: Scroll
tagline: Native zkEVM Layer-2 for Ethereum
description: Scroll is a zero-knowledge rollup on Ethereum that executes the full EVM at the bytecode level, generating validity proofs per batch and posting data and proofs back to mainnet. Co-founded in 2021 by Sandy Peng, Ye Zhang and Haichen Shen, its mainnet launched in October 2023. USDC and USDT are available natively via canonical bridges, and the ecosystem includes USX, a private gasless native stablecoin, with a yield-bearing version sUSX.
notable: Backed by Polychain, Sequoia China, Bain Capital Crypto and Variant at a $1.8B valuation.

name: Securitize
tagline: Tokenization platform behind BlackRock BUIDL
description: Securitize is the leading RWA tokenization and transfer-agent platform, issuing and administering tokenized funds for BlackRock (BUIDL), Apollo, Hamilton Lane, KKR and VanEck. It is an SEC-registered broker-dealer and transfer agent and the largest player in the tokenization industry by AUM (over $4B, ~20% market share).
notable: Issuance and compliance partner for the largest tokenized fund, BlackRock BUIDL.

name: Sei Network
tagline: The Layer-1 blockchain for trading
description: Sei is a high-performance Layer-1 blockchain optimized for trading, founded by Jayendra Jog (ex-Robinhood) and Jeff Feng (ex-Coatue) and launched in August 2023 with the SEI token. Its Sei V2 upgrade added a parallelized EVM with optimistic parallelization and SeiDB, delivering thousands of TPS with sub-400ms finality. Circle made a strategic investment in Sei and bridged USDC to it, with native USDC and CCTP V2 integrated by 2025; Ondo's USDY tokenized-Treasury fund also launched on Sei.
funding: $30M Series A (2023); strategic investment from Circle Ventures
notable: Received strategic investment from Circle; native USDC and CCTP V2 integrated.

name: Sentiment
tagline: Leverage lending protocol
description: Sentiment is a leveraged lending protocol designed for complex on-chain portfolio positions, with adaptive risk management and permissionless pool creation supporting various collateral types. Its newer iteration is built for high-throughput chains like Hyperliquid, and it supports stablecoin lending and borrowing as part of leverage strategies.
notable: Specialized in leveraged, portfolio-margin lending.

name: Sentora
tagline: Institutional DeFi yield, liquidity and risk platform
description: Sentora is an institutional-grade DeFi asset management platform formed in 2025 from the merger of analytics firm IntoTheBlock and structured-liquidity provider Trident Digital. It combines IntoTheBlock's onchain analytics with Trident's structured liquidity solutions, operating non-custodial vaults with real-time analytics and over $3B deployed across protocols and chains. Sentora targets compliant institutional access to DeFi yield, liquidity provisioning and risk management, with relationships across ecosystems including ether.fi and Aave.
notable: Formed from the merger of IntoTheBlock and Trident Digital with $25M Series A backing.

name: Sequence
tagline: Non-custodial smart wallets for production payments
description: Sequence delivers non-custodial smart wallets for ecosystems and apps, with passkeys, social auth, timed recovery keys and sandboxed permissions via Smart Sessions. Acquired by Polygon Labs in 2026 (with Coinme) to anchor the Open Money Stack for regulated stablecoin payments.
notable: Acquired by Polygon Labs in 2026 for stablecoin payments.

name: Shopify
tagline: Commerce platform enabling USDC checkout for merchants
description: Shopify is a global commerce platform that, via Shopify Payments, enabled millions of merchants across 30+ countries to accept USDC on Base — powered by Coinbase and Stripe — with automatic conversion to local currency and no FX fees. It uses a purpose-built escrow smart contract for auth/capture.
notable: Rolled out native USDC-on-Base checkout to millions of merchants.

name: Signature Bank
tagline: Former crypto banking leader (Signet); failed 2023
description: Signature Bank was a leading US crypto-friendly bank whose Signet real-time payments network served stablecoin and digital-asset clients. It was historically significant for stablecoin banking before its March 2023 failure and seizure by regulators.
notable: Signet was a leading stablecoin banking rail before the 2023 failure.

name: Silo Finance
tagline: Isolated-market lending
description: Silo is a non-custodial lending protocol built on risk-isolated markets, where each asset is paired in its own silo to contain contagion. Silo V2 enables permissionless market creation, and stablecoins are core bridge and borrow assets. It is a notable challenger in risk-isolated lending, prominent on Sonic and Ethereum.
notable: Risk-isolated lending markets.

name: Silvergate Bank
tagline: Former crypto bank (SEN); wound down 2023
description: Silvergate was a pioneering US crypto bank whose Silvergate Exchange Network (SEN) provided real-time fiat settlement for exchanges and stablecoin issuers. It was historically central to stablecoin banking before voluntarily winding down in 2023.
notable: SEN was a foundational stablecoin/exchange settlement rail before 2023 wind-down.

name: Simplex (Nuvei)
tagline: Fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp by Nuvei
description: Simplex, acquired by payments giant Nuvei, is a long-standing fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp with chargeback liability coverage and card acquiring, integrated into many exchanges and wallets worldwide.
notable: Owned by listed PSP Nuvei.

name: Skip / Interchain Labs (IBC Eureka)
tagline: IBC interoperability for Cosmos and Ethereum
description: Skip (now part of Interchain Labs) builds Skip Go cross-chain routing and IBC Eureka, the canonical IBC v2 implementation that extends Inter-Blockchain Communication between 115+ Cosmos chains and Ethereum. It enables trust-minimized cross-chain stablecoin transfers via light-client security.
notable: IBC Eureka connects Cosmos to Ethereum via light clients (2025).

name: Sky
tagline: MakerDAO rebrand; USDS issuer
description: Sky (the rebrand of MakerDAO) issues USDS and DAI, decentralized stablecoins, and runs the Sky Savings Rate paid on sUSDS. As the original DeFi credit system (the Maker Vault / collateralized debt position model), Sky remains a foundational lending and stablecoin protocol, channeling billions into RWAs and DeFi via its SubDAO stars including Spark.
notable: Originator of the DeFi CDP/lending model; issues USDS and DAI.

name: Skyfire
tagline: Payment rails and identity for AI agents
description: Skyfire builds the agent trust stack: a payments network and Know Your Agent (KYA) identity layer that lets autonomous AI agents transact using stablecoins. Funds deposited in USD are held as USDC in agent-tied wallets, with programmable spending and tokenized payment cards for machine-led commerce across APIs and digital services.
notable: Founded by ex-Ripple executives; pitched as the 'PayPal for AI agents'. Partnered with Fastly on edge agent payments.

name: Slash
tagline: Business banking, corporate cards and stablecoin payments
description: Slash is a multi-product business banking platform combining FDIC-insured checking, corporate cards, expense and treasury management, global payments and stablecoin support. It powers over $30B in annualized payment volume across 5,000+ businesses and surpassed $1B in annualized stablecoin payment volume within nine months of launching the product. It reached unicorn status with a $100M Series C in 2026.
funding: $160M+ total; $100M Series C (2026) led by Ribbit Capital at $1.4B valuation
notable: Exceeded $1B annualized stablecoin payment volume within 9 months of launch.

name: Sling Money
tagline: Stablecoin-powered global money transfer app
description: Sling Money, built by Avian Labs, is a consumer app for sending digital dollars and euros worldwide in seconds using USDP and EURC on Solana, with free wallet transfers in 145+ countries and instant local-currency withdrawals in 80 countries.
funding: $20M total; $15M Series A led by Union Square Ventures and Slow Ventures
notable: Holds MiCA (Netherlands) and FCA approvals; also operates as Morse.

name: Societe Generale FORGE
tagline: Bank-issued euro and dollar stablecoins (EURCV, USDCV)
description: SG-FORGE, the regulated digital-assets subsidiary of Societe Generale, issues the euro stablecoin EUR CoinVertible (EURCV) and the US-dollar USD CoinVertible (USDCV) on Ethereum and Solana, with BNY as reserve custodian — among the first major-bank stablecoins.
notable: Among the first major banks to issue regulated EUR/USD stablecoins.

name: Socket
tagline: Chain-abstraction interoperability protocol
description: Socket is an interoperability and chain-abstraction protocol that aggregates routing across bridges (Across, Stargate, Hop, Celer, Connext) and DEXs via a unified API. It builds the consumer-facing Bungee aggregator and is developing Socket Protocol for modular chain abstraction.
notable: Powers the Bungee bridge aggregator; broad bridge routing coverage.

name: SoFi
tagline: Issuer of SoFiUSD, the first US national bank stablecoin
description: SoFi Technologies is a publicly traded US digital bank that in December 2025 launched SoFiUSD, a fully reserved US-dollar stablecoin redeemable 1:1 through SoFi Bank. It is positioned as the first stablecoin issued by a US national bank to launch on a public, permissionless blockchain (Ethereum and Solana) and is available directly in the SoFi app to nearly 15 million members. SoFi also offers SoFiUSD as white-label stablecoin infrastructure to banks, fintechs and enterprises.
notable: First US national bank to issue a stablecoin on a public, permissionless blockchain, made available to ~15 million members.

name: Solana
tagline: High-throughput L1 with major stablecoin volume
description: Solana is a high-performance Layer-1 known for low fees and fast finality, and a leading venue for stablecoin payments and high-frequency USDC/USDT flows. It is a primary chain for consumer payments apps, DePIN, and stablecoin-settled trading, with native USDC and PYUSD support.
notable: Leading chain for fast, low-cost stablecoin payments and trading volume.

name: Solayer Emerald Card
tagline: Solana DeFi-yield spending card
description: The Solayer Emerald Card combines DeFi yield with spending rewards on Solana, letting users earn yield on stablecoin balances while spending at merchants, aimed at Solayer's crypto-native community.
notable: Solana yield-bearing spending card.

name: Solflare
tagline: Solana-native non-custodial wallet
description: Solflare is a long-standing Solana-native self-custody wallet supporting staking, swaps, NFTs and stablecoins like USDC and USDT, available across mobile, extension and web with hardware-wallet support.
notable: Major Solana-native consumer wallet.

name: Solidus Labs
tagline: Crypto-native market surveillance and risk monitoring
description: Solidus Labs provides crypto-native trade surveillance, market manipulation detection, transaction monitoring and risk monitoring (HALO) for exchanges, brokers, asset managers and regulators, addressing both AML and market-integrity risk.
notable: Leading crypto market-surveillance/manipulation detection.

name: Sommelier
tagline: Automated DeFi strategy vaults
description: Sommelier is a Cosmos-based protocol coordinating actively managed DeFi vaults ('Cellars') that dynamically reallocate assets, including stablecoin yield strategies, across Ethereum DeFi. It enables sophisticated, gas-efficient automated strategies for stablecoin and LST yield.
notable: Actively managed cross-chain DeFi strategy vaults.

name: Soneium
tagline: Sony's Ethereum Layer 2
description: Soneium is an Ethereum Layer 2 built on the OP Stack and launched by Sony Block Solutions Labs, a joint venture between Sony Group and Startale. It targets entertainment, gaming and consumer applications, has Circle's USDC as a primary token, and gained Startale USD (USDSC), an M0-powered institutional stablecoin, in late 2025.
funding: Backed by Sony Group via Sony Block Solutions Labs
notable: Sony's own Ethereum L2; first institutional stablecoin USDSC launched via Startale and M0 in Dec 2025.

name: Sonic
tagline: Fast EVM Layer-1, the rebranded Fantom
description: Sonic (formerly Fantom) is a high-performance EVM Layer-1 blockchain that launched its mainnet in December 2024, capable of ~10,000 TPS with sub-second finality and a 90% fee-sharing model rewarding developers. Sonic Labs introduced USSD, a US dollar stablecoin built on Frax Finance infrastructure backed 1:1 by short-duration US Treasuries via BlackRock, Superstate and WisdomTree, mintable cross-chain from 10+ blockchains. In 2025 the community approved a $150M token issuance to fund a US capital-markets expansion including an ETF initiative.
notable: Rebrand of Fantom; USSD stablecoin built on Frax infrastructure backed by US Treasuries.

name: Sony Bank
tagline: Japanese digital bank piloting yen-backed stablecoins
description: Sony Bank is the digital banking arm of Sony Group that has been piloting a yen-pegged stablecoin, including an earlier trial on Polygon aimed at gaming and entertainment IP payments. It signed an MoU with JPYC Inc. to enable real-time purchase of the JPYC yen stablecoin directly from Sony Bank deposits, with its Web3 subsidiary BlockBloom designing the integration between bank rails and stablecoin rails.
notable: Piloting deposit-layer yen stablecoin integration with JPYC for entertainment and gaming payments.

name: Spec
tagline: Real-time fraud and compliance defense
description: Spec provides a real-time fraud, abuse and compliance defense platform using session and behavioral signals to stop account takeover, onboarding fraud and money-laundering attempts, used by fintech, crypto and stablecoin on-ramp companies.
notable: Session-level behavioral fraud and compliance defense.

name: Sphere
tagline: Global stablecoin payments API and dashboard
description: Sphere (SpherePay) is a B2B payments platform and API suite helping fintechs and institutions move value across borders compliantly, supporting on/off-ramp flows, multi-chain stablecoin transfers, white-label embeds and SDKs. It is also building SphereNet, a payments network.
notable: Partnered with Nexo to advance cross-border business payments.

name: Spiko
tagline: Europe's first tokenized money-market funds
description: Spiko is a Paris-based fintech that launched Europe's first AMF-approved tokenized money-market funds (EUTBL in euros, USTBL in dollars), giving businesses on-chain access to European and US sovereign-debt yield. It scaled to hundreds of millions in AUM in its first year.
notable: First fully tokenized MMFs approved by France's AMF.

name: Squads
tagline: Smart account and multisig infrastructure for Solana
description: Squads is the leading smart-contract wallet and multisig infrastructure layer for Solana and the SVM, securing over $10B in assets and processing $3B+ in stablecoin transfers. Its programs power treasury management, account abstraction, embedded smart accounts (Altitude) and programmable stablecoin payments and payroll (Grid) for DAOs, companies and developers.
notable: Secures $10B+ in assets as Solana's leading multisig.

name: Squid Router
tagline: Cross-chain swap and bridge built on Axelar
description: Squid is a cross-chain liquidity router built on Axelar's GMP that lets users and apps swap and bridge in a single transaction across 100+ chains. Its CORAL intent engine and Squid Coral SDK power one-click cross-chain stablecoin movement embedded in wallets and dApps.
funding: Axelar ecosystem; backed by 1kx, North Island
notable: One-click swap-and-bridge across 100+ chains via Axelar GMP.

name: Stable
tagline: USDT-gas L1 for stablecoin payments
description: Stable is a Layer-1 blockchain purpose-built for stablecoin payments that uses USDT itself as the native gas asset, with no separate L1 token required for fees. Associated with the USDT0 cross-chain transport team and positioned as a Tether-aligned settlement layer for remittances and merchant flows, it added PYUSD support with PayPal Ventures and targets a 2026 mainnet.
notable: Uses USDT directly as gas; integrated by payment platforms like Orbital.

name: Stable Sea
tagline: Low-cost B2B cross-border stablecoin payments
description: Stable Sea is a B2B cross-border payments provider using stablecoin rails to lower costs and speed settlement, partnering with dLocal to combine stablecoin liquidity with local payment infrastructure across emerging markets.
notable: Partnered with dLocal in 2025 for low-cost B2B stablecoin payments.

name: Stables
tagline: Stablecoin money app and card
description: Stables is an Australia-based stablecoin money app and card backed by USDT/USDC, letting users top up with stablecoins and spend at 140M+ merchants globally. It also provides USDt payments infrastructure for Asia and MENA.
notable: Tether-aligned stablecoin card and payments app for APAC/MENA.

name: StablR
tagline: Issuer of EURR and USDR
description: StablR is a Malta-based, MFSA-supervised issuer of the MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin EURR and USD stablecoin USDR, with reserves and custody arrangements aimed at institutional payment use cases.
notable: Maltese MiCA-compliant euro and dollar issuer.

name: Stably
tagline: Multi-currency stablecoin-as-a-service platform
description: Stably is a stablecoin-as-a-service platform providing multi-currency, fiat-backed stablecoin issuance with integrated fiat on/off-ramps. It has served as a primary stablecoin partner for the Algorand and Stacks blockchains, enabling branded and ecosystem stablecoins.
notable: Early stablecoin-as-a-service provider; Algorand/Stacks partner.

name: StakeStone
tagline: Issuer of USDz/STONE omnichain yield assets
description: StakeStone is an omnichain liquidity protocol issuing yield-bearing assets including a yield-bearing stablecoin (USDz/SBTC suite alongside its STONE LST), distributing native yield across multiple chains via its omnichain framework.
funding: Backed by Binance Labs, OKX Ventures
notable: Omnichain distribution of yield-bearing assets.

name: Standard Chartered
tagline: Global bank providing custody and reserve banking for digital assets
description: Standard Chartered is a global bank expanding into digital assets, providing custody and reserve banking. It is bringing its crypto custodian Zodia Custody fully into core operations and partners with stablecoin and tokenization initiatives across Asia, the Middle East and Europe.
notable: Acquiring full control of crypto custodian Zodia Custody.

name: Standard Custody & Trust
tagline: NYDFS-chartered trust custodian (PolySign)
description: Standard Custody & Trust Company is a NYDFS-chartered qualified custodian (part of PolySign) providing institutional cold custody and settlement. It serves as a regulated custodian and reserve partner for tokenized assets and stablecoins.
notable: NYDFS-chartered trust company.

name: Standard Economics
tagline: Stablecoin cross-border payments app (Uno)
description: Standard Economics is a stablecoin payments startup building Uno, a cross-border payments and remittance app that gives users USD access via stablecoins. Uno first launched in Mexico in October 2025 with domestic payments and zero-fee cross-border transfers, with plans to expand to Argentina, the Philippines and other Latin American and Asian markets.
notable: Co-founded by Evan Jones, Payam Abedi and Tyler Carnevale; Paradigm-led seed.

name: Stargate Finance
tagline: Omnichain liquidity transport built on LayerZero
description: Stargate is a fully-composable liquidity transport protocol built on LayerZero that enables native asset transfers (including stablecoins) across chains using unified liquidity pools with guaranteed instant finality. It is one of the highest-volume bridges for USDC and USDT.
funding: STG token; backed by LayerZero ecosystem
notable: Flagship LayerZero-based stablecoin bridge with deep USDC/USDT liquidity.

name: Starknet
tagline: Ethereum ZK-rollup scaling network
description: Starknet is a permissionless ZK-rollup (validity rollup) Layer 2 on Ethereum developed by StarkWare, using STARK proofs and the Cairo programming language to deliver low-cost, high-throughput transactions. It hosts a growing DeFi ecosystem and supports major dollar stablecoins including USDC and USDT used for trading, lending and payments.
funding: StarkWare raised over $260M; backers include Paradigm, Sequoia, Pantera
notable: Pioneered STARK-based validity rollups and the Cairo language for provable computation.

name: Startale Group
tagline: Japanese Web3 infrastructure firm issuing yen and dollar stablecoins
description: Startale Group is a Japan-based Web3 infrastructure company that operates Astar Network and co-develops the Sony-backed Soneium L2 via Sony Block Solutions Labs. In partnership with SBI Holdings it is launching JPYSC, the first trust-bank-backed JPY stablecoin (issued by Shinsei Trust & Banking), alongside a dollar stablecoin USDSC and the tokenized-asset chain Strium. It raised a $63M Series A backed by SBI Group and Sony Innovation Fund.
funding: $63M Series A (SBI Group, Sony Innovation Fund)
notable: Co-developer of Sony's Soneium L2; building Japan's first trust-bank-backed yen stablecoin with SBI.

name: Stasis
tagline: Issuer of EURS, the largest euro-backed stablecoin
description: Stasis is a Malta-based stablecoin issuer that launched EURS in 2018, one of the largest and most established euro-backed stablecoins. EURS is fully collateralized 1:1 by euro bank deposits and liquid financial instruments, with daily reserve statements, quarterly verifications and annual audits by BDO Malta, and is available across multiple blockchains including Ethereum, Polygon, Algorand, Stellar and XDC.
notable: EURS is among the longest-running and largest euro-backed stablecoins, launched in 2018.

name: State Street
tagline: Custodian bank and asset manager targeting stablecoin reserves
description: State Street is a major global custodian bank and asset manager. It holds portions of stablecoin reserves (including for Paxos) and in 2026 launched a government money market fund built specifically for stablecoin issuers seeking GENIUS Act-eligible reserve assets.
notable: Launched a dedicated stablecoin reserves MMF in 2026.

name: Steakhouse Financial
tagline: Crypto-native financial advisory and DeFi risk curator
description: Steakhouse Financial is a crypto-native financial advisory and risk-curation firm founded in 2020, advising leading protocols including Sky (MakerDAO), Coinbase, Lido and Ethena on treasury, ALM and RWA strategy. It helped onboard US Treasuries and private credit to MakerDAO and is now the largest curator on Morpho, managing dozens of vaults across multiple chains. It incubated Grove.
notable: Largest curator on Morpho; helped bring RWAs to MakerDAO.

name: Stellar
tagline: Payments L1 for cross-border stablecoin settlement
description: Stellar is a Layer-1 designed for fast, low-cost payments and asset issuance, widely used for cross-border stablecoin settlement and remittances. It hosts USDC and EURC and powers Visa/Wirex card stablecoin settlement and x402 agentic payments, with an anchor network bridging fiat on/off ramps globally.
notable: Established cross-border payments rail with a global fiat anchor network.

name: Stork
tagline: Ultra-low-latency price oracle
description: Stork delivers price data at ultra-low latency for perpetuals markets, lending protocols, and DeFi ecosystems, rapidly supporting new assets on listing. Its pull-based architecture targets high-frequency trading and derivatives use cases requiring sub-second updates.
notable: Focused on ultra-low-latency feeds for perps and derivatives.

name: StraitsX
tagline: Issuer of XSGD, XUSD and XIDR
description: StraitsX is a Singapore-based stablecoin and payments infrastructure provider, licensed as a Major Payment Institution by the Monetary Authority of Singapore. It issues XSGD, the Singapore-dollar stablecoin launched in 2020 and backed 1:1 by reserves held with DBS Bank and Standard Chartered, alongside XUSD. XSGD operates across Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Hedera, Zilliqa and the XRP Ledger, with monthly reserve attestations, and the company is expanding both stablecoins onto Solana.
notable: Leading Singapore-regulated multi-currency stablecoin issuer.

name: Stream Finance
tagline: Collapsed yield stablecoin (xUSD) at center of 2025 DeFi contagion
description: Stream Finance issued xUSD, a yield-bearing 'stablecoin' marketed with ~18% APY backed by leveraged and off-chain strategies. On November 4, 2025 it disclosed a ~$93M loss tied to an external fund manager; xUSD crashed roughly 77-82% from $1 toward $0.20s, freezing ~$160M in deposits and triggering over $285M of contagion across DeFi, including the near-total collapse of Elixir's deUSD. The protocol is effectively insolvent.
notable: Its November 2025 collapse caused $285M+ of DeFi contagion.

name: Stripe
tagline: Global payments platform with native stablecoin checkout
description: Stripe is the dominant internet payments platform. After acquiring stablecoin infrastructure startup Bridge for ~$1.1B in early 2025 and stablecoin wallet firm Privy, Stripe rolled out native stablecoin acceptance: every Stripe merchant can accept USDC (and USDB) through standard checkout, and Stripe powers USDC payments for millions of Shopify merchants. It charges a flat ~1.5% on stablecoin transactions.
notable: Acquired Bridge (~$1.1B) to become a stablecoin payments powerhouse; enables USDC checkout for Shopify merchants.

name: Stronghold
tagline: Blockchain payments infrastructure powered by SHx
description: Stronghold is a financial infrastructure company building payments and settlement rails on the Stellar network, powered by its SHx utility token. It has issued Stronghold USD, a US-dollar-backed stablecoin on Stellar, and provides embedded payments and settlement services for enterprises, with SHx tradable against Circle's USDC on Stellar's DEX and a bridge to the XRP Ledger.
notable: Issued one of the earliest USD stablecoins on Stellar.

name: Sui
tagline: Move L1 with native USDC and growing stablecoin payments
description: Sui is a high-performance Layer-1 built with the Move language, offering parallel execution and low fees. It supports native USDC and a growing set of stablecoins, targeting consumer payments, gaming, and DeFi liquidity.
notable: Parallel-execution Move L1 with native USDC support.

name: Summer.fi
tagline: DeFi access layer for borrowing, multiply and yield
description: Summer.fi (formerly Oasis.app, originating from the Maker ecosystem) is a non-custodial DeFi front-end and aggregator for borrowing, leveraged Multiply positions, and automated Lazy Summer yield strategies across protocols like Sky/Maker, Aave, Spark and Morpho. It is a major interface for managing stablecoin debt (DAI/USDS) and earning yield.
funding: Raised funding incl. backing from RockawayX and others
notable: Evolved from MakerDAO's Oasis.app into a multi-protocol DeFi access layer.

name: Sumsub
tagline: Full-cycle verification and AML platform
description: Sumsub provides KYC/KYB identity verification, AML screening, fraud prevention and crypto transaction monitoring (powered partly by integrations with blockchain analytics vendors). It is widely used by crypto exchanges, on/off-ramps and fintechs for onboarding and ongoing compliance.
notable: Leading identity verification and AML suite for crypto onboarding.

name: Superfluid
tagline: Stream money every second on-chain
description: Superfluid is a money-streaming protocol enabling real-time, continuous on-chain cashflows via 'Super Tokens', an extension of ERC-20. A single transaction creates a per-second stream used by crypto-native organizations for salaries, grants, subscriptions and rewards, and works with stablecoins and other tokens.
notable: Pioneer of per-second token streaming for recurring on-chain payments.

name: Superset
tagline: Unified liquidity execution layer for stablecoins and onchain FX
description: Superset is a cross-chain stablecoin liquidity and onchain FX infrastructure project that consolidates fragmented stablecoin liquidity into one open onchain market. It uses a hub-and-spoke model that routes pricing and execution through a central chain (Arbitrum) while settling trades across multiple networks, targeting consistent fills and clearer pricing for LPs, market makers, issuers and wallets.
funding: $4M seed led by 7RIDGE and Exponential Science Capital
notable: Former ClearBank CEO Charles McManus joined as chairman and co-investor.

name: Superstate
tagline: Issuer of USTB and USCC tokenized yield funds
description: Superstate, founded by Compound creator Robert Leshner, issues USTB (tokenized short-term Treasuries) and USCC (crypto carry fund) as yield-bearing on-chain investment tokens for institutions. It bridges regulated funds and DeFi composability.
funding: $14M+ Series A (Distributed Global, others) plus later rounds
notable: Founded by Compound's Robert Leshner; tokenized regulated yield funds.

name: Supra
tagline: Vertically integrated oracle and cross-chain toolkit
description: Supra is a vertically integrated toolkit of cross-chain solutions providing decentralized oracle price feeds, VRF, and cross-chain interoperability for both on-chain and off-chain use cases. It targets high-throughput, low-latency data delivery across many L1s and L2s.
notable: Combines oracle, VRF, and bridging in a single vertically integrated stack.

name: SushiSwap
tagline: Multi-chain DEX and DeFi suite
description: SushiSwap is a long-running decentralized exchange and DeFi platform deployed across 30+ chains. It offers AMM swaps, concentrated liquidity (v3), a cross-chain swap aggregator (SushiXSwap), lending and yield products, making it a significant venue for stablecoin liquidity and routing.
notable: One of the original DeFi blue-chip DEXs, now multi-chain with cross-chain swaps.

name: Swapped.com
tagline: European fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp
description: Swapped.com is a European-focused fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp offering instant buy/sell with cards, SEPA, and local rails, available as a consumer product and partner ramp.
notable: SEPA-native European ramp.

name: Swarm
tagline: Regulated DeFi for tokenized stocks and bonds
description: Swarm (Swarm Markets) is a BaFin-regulated platform offering tokenized public equities (e.g. AAPL, TSLA) and tokenized US Treasuries that can be traded and used in DeFi. It bridges regulated securities into permissionless markets.
notable: BaFin-regulated DeFi venue for tokenized securities.

name: Switchboard
tagline: Permissionless customizable oracle
description: Switchboard is a permissionless oracle network letting developers build custom data feeds for price data, randomness, and verticals from insurance verification to gaming. It originated on Solana and uses a TEE-based architecture (Switchboard On-Demand) for low-latency, customizable feeds across multiple chains.
notable: Solana-native customizable oracle with TEE-based on-demand feeds.

name: Sygnum
tagline: Regulated digital asset bank with custody
description: Sygnum is a Swiss and Singapore-licensed digital asset bank offering institutional custody, brokerage, tokenization and yield. As a chartered bank it provides bankruptcy-remote regulated custody and is a custody/reserve counterparty for tokenized assets and stablecoins.
notable: First digital asset bank in the world.

name: Symbiosis
tagline: Cross-chain liquidity and swap protocol
description: Symbiosis is a cross-chain AMM and liquidity protocol that enables swaps and stablecoin transfers between EVM and non-EVM chains (including Bitcoin, Tron, and TON) through a single interface. It uses a decentralized relayer network and stable pools for cross-chain settlement.
notable: Connects EVM and non-EVM chains incl. Tron, TON, Bitcoin.

name: Synapse Protocol
tagline: Cross-chain bridge with stableswap liquidity
description: Synapse is a cross-chain communications protocol and bridge that runs Curve-style stableswap pools across chains, offering one of the broadest lists of supported stablecoins and chains. Its Synapse Chain and Interchain Network extend generic messaging beyond simple asset transfers.
notable: Broad stablecoin and chain coverage via cross-chain stableswap.

name: Synthetix
tagline: Issuer of sUSD, the SNX-backed synthetic dollar
description: Synthetix is a veteran Ethereum and Optimism DeFi protocol best known for sUSD, a crypto-collateralized stablecoin minted against staked SNX, and for its perpetual futures liquidity. In April 2025 sUSD depegged to as low as ~$0.66 after SIP-420 cut minter collateralization from 750% to 200% via a shared debt pool; the team has since pursued buybacks and incentive changes to rebuild the peg.
notable: One of DeFi's earliest synthetic-asset protocols; sUSD suffered a major depeg in 2025.

name: t3rn
tagline: Universal cross-chain execution protocol
description: t3rn is a cross-chain interoperability protocol providing a Universal Execution Protocol for fail-safe, atomic multi-chain smart contract execution across EVM and non-EVM chains. Off-chain attestors validate and settle cross-chain transactions so multi-step operations either fully complete or revert, ensuring funds are never lost. It supports ERC-7683 intents and is used for cross-chain swaps and bridging.
notable: Received a strategic grant from the Arbitrum Foundation; an early implementer of the ERC-7683 cross-chain intents standard.

name: Tangany
tagline: White-label custody-as-a-service (Germany)
description: Tangany is a German BaFin-licensed custody-as-a-service provider offering API-based white-label custody and tokenization infrastructure to banks, fintechs and enterprises. It enables regulated custody and stablecoin/token issuance without building in-house key management.
notable: BaFin-licensed white-label custody for banks and fintechs.

name: Tangible (Real USD)
tagline: Defunct real-estate-backed yield stablecoin (USDR)
description: Tangible issued Real USD (USDR) on Polygon, a yield-bearing stablecoin backed largely by tokenized real estate plus a DAI liquidity buffer. In October 2023 USDR depegged to roughly $0.51 after a redemption rush drained its liquid DAI, leaving holders facing illiquid real-estate-backed assets. Tangible subsequently wound down USDR and offered compensation via DAI and real-estate-pool tokens.
notable: USDR depegged ~50% in October 2023 and was wound down.

name: Tassat
tagline: Tokenized deposits & real-time settlement infrastructure
description: Tassat provides tokenized-deposit and real-time B2B settlement infrastructure for banks, with its TassatPay network and Digital Interbank Network enabling instant, 24/7 dollar settlement and tokenized money movement — adjacent issuance/orchestration rails for institutional dollar transfers.
notable: Bank-grade tokenized deposit settlement rails.

name: Taurus
tagline: Swiss enterprise custody and tokenization infrastructure
description: Taurus is a Swiss digital asset infrastructure provider offering custody (Taurus-PROTECT), tokenization (Taurus-CAPITAL) and a regulated marketplace (TDX). Backed by Credit Suisse and Deutsche Bank, it provides white-label custody and tokenization to banks across Europe and is a key enterprise platform for regulated digital assets and stablecoins.
funding: $65M Series B led by Credit Suisse
notable: Banking-grade custody used by major European banks.

name: Tazapay
tagline: Cross-border B2B collections and payouts with stablecoin settlement
description: Tazapay is a Singapore-based cross-border payments platform connecting stablecoin settlement to B2B collection and payout corridors across Asia and emerging markets, with local payment method coverage in 170+ markets.
funding: $16.9M Series A (2023), led by Sequoia Southeast Asia
notable: Focus on Asia-centric cross-border B2B corridors.

name: Tellor
tagline: Permissionless decentralized oracle
description: Tellor is a permissionless, Ethereum-based oracle where reporters stake TRB to submit data and submissions can be disputed on-chain. It emphasizes censorship resistance and decentralization for price and arbitrary data feeds used by DeFi protocols.
notable: Permissionless staking/dispute oracle prioritizing decentralization.

name: Tempo
tagline: Stablecoin-native payments L1 backed by Stripe and Paradigm
description: Tempo is an EVM-compatible Layer-1 blockchain purpose-built for stablecoin payments, incubated by Stripe and Paradigm and spun out as an independent company. It targets enterprise and merchant payments with stablecoins as the default gas asset, leveraging Stripe's distribution, Bridge issuance infrastructure, and Privy wallets. It opened a public testnet in late 2025 ahead of a 2026 mainnet.
funding: Series A ~$500M at ~$5B valuation (Thrive Capital, Greenoaks, Paradigm)
notable: Stripe + Paradigm collaboration; completes Stripe's full-stack stablecoin lifecycle (Bridge, Privy).

name: Tencent / WeChat Pay
tagline: China's dominant mobile payments super-app
description: WeChat Pay, operated by Tencent's Tenpay subsidiary within the WeChat super-app, is one of China's two dominant mobile payment networks with over a billion users. It handles QR-based in-store and online payments, cross-border merchant acceptance, and is exploring digital-currency and tokenized-payment initiatives, though Chinese regulation has kept it focused on fiat and the e-CNY rather than public stablecoins.
notable: One of the two largest mobile payment networks in the world by users.

name: Term Finance
tagline: Fixed-rate, fixed-term DeFi lending
description: Term Finance is a non-custodial protocol for fixed-rate, fixed-term lending and borrowing, using a weekly auction to match stablecoin lenders and borrowers. It brings predictable bond-like rates to onchain stablecoin credit, differentiating from variable-rate money markets.
notable: Auction-based fixed-rate onchain lending.

name: Tether
tagline: Issuer of USDT, the largest stablecoin
description: Tether issues USDT, the world's largest dollar stablecoin with the dominant share of total stablecoin market cap. It also issues EURT, the gold-backed XAUT, and the US-regulated USAT. Tether's reserves are heavily allocated to US Treasuries and it has become one of the most profitable companies in crypto.
notable: Largest stablecoin issuer by market cap and circulating supply.

name: Texas Capital Bank
tagline: US bank backing stablecoin reserves
description: Texas Capital Bank is a Texas-based commercial bank serving as a reserve and banking partner for stablecoin issuers, providing custody of reserve cash and banking infrastructure for digital-dollar issuance under US regulatory frameworks.
notable: Among the US banks providing reserve and banking services to stablecoin issuers.

name: The Better Money Company
tagline: Clearing layer for stablecoins
description: The Better Money Company is building a stablecoin clearinghouse that lets any stablecoin be exchanged for any other at par, with no slippage, across chains and issuers. Rather than routing through liquidity pools or OTC desks, it settles 1:1 via direct participation from banking partners and issuers. Founded by ex-a16z crypto investor Sam Broner and Adam Zuckerman, it raised a $10M seed round led by a16z crypto in 2026.
notable: Building the 'missing connectivity layer' for stablecoins; prospective participants include Paxos, Bridge, MoonPay, Agora, M0, Frax, Brale, MetaMask and Phantom.

name: The Graph
tagline: Decentralized indexing and query protocol
description: The Graph is a decentralized protocol for indexing and querying blockchain data via GraphQL subgraphs. Indexers, curators and delegators stake GRT to serve verifiable on-chain data to dApps and analytics tools, including data used to track stablecoin flows, balances and contract activity.
notable: Leading decentralized blockchain data indexing protocol.

name: Theo Network
tagline: Aggregated institutional Treasury yield (thBILL)
description: Theo Network offers thBILL, an index of institutional-grade tokenized Treasury products, aggregating exposure (e.g. Wellington-managed tULTRA via Libeara) and wrapping it with continuous market-making and DeFi distribution. It scaled to $200M+ TVL within months of launch.
notable: Aggregates institutional tokenized Treasuries with DeFi liquidity.

name: Thirdweb
tagline: Full-stack Web3 dev platform with embedded wallets
description: Thirdweb is a one-stop Web3 development platform offering smart contracts, embedded/in-app wallets, account abstraction, and infrastructure. Its in-app wallets support email/social login and are widely used to onboard mainstream users to stablecoin-enabled apps.
notable: One-stop-shop for contracts, wallets and infrastructure.

name: THORChain
tagline: Native cross-chain liquidity protocol
description: THORChain is a decentralized cross-chain liquidity network that enables native asset swaps across major chains (Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB) without wrapping, with continuous liquidity pools and its own stablecoin TOR. It is a major venue for non-custodial cross-chain swaps including stablecoins.
notable: Native cross-chain swaps without wrapped assets.

name: Threshold (thUSD)
tagline: BTC and ETH-backed stablecoin thUSD
description: Threshold Network issues thUSD, an overcollateralized stablecoin (a Liquity-derived design) backed by tBTC and ETH, bringing Bitcoin collateral into a decentralized CDP stablecoin model.
notable: Uses tBTC as stablecoin collateral.

name: Thunes
tagline: Global cross-border payments network with stablecoin reach
description: Thunes operates a global cross-border payments network reaching billions of mobile wallets and bank accounts, expanding into stablecoin settlement to make cross-border payouts faster and more accessible in emerging markets.
funding: $72M Series C (2023), backed by Marshall Wace, Bessemer
notable: Connecting stablecoins and mobile wallets for cross-border access.

name: Timeswap
tagline: Oracle-less, permissionless lending protocol
description: Timeswap is the first oracle-less lending and borrowing protocol, enabling permissionless creation of fixed-maturity money markets for any ERC-20 / ERC-4626 pair via a novel 3-variable constant-product AMM. Its non-liquidatable, oracle-free design supports markets for long-tail and stablecoin assets across multiple chains.
funding: Raised seed funding incl. Multicoin Capital and Mechanism Capital
notable: Oracle-less and non-liquidatable lending design — 'Uniswap for lending'.

name: Tokemak
tagline: Liquidity routing and autonomous liquidity management
description: Tokemak is a liquidity management protocol that optimizes and routes liquidity across DeFi. Its autopilot/autopools (v2) automatically allocate deposited assets to the best-yielding liquidity pools, including stablecoin (autoUSD) and ETH strategies, abstracting away manual LP management and rebalancing.
funding: Raised funding incl. backing from Framework Ventures and others
notable: Pioneered protocol-controlled liquidity; v2 automates yield-optimized LP routing.

name: Token Terminal
tagline: Financial data platform for crypto
description: Token Terminal is a financial and on-chain data platform that aggregates standardized metrics for blockchains, protocols and applications, including stablecoin supply, transfer volume, revenue and active-address data. Its dashboards and APIs are widely used by analysts and institutions to track stablecoin issuers and DeFi activity.
funding: Venture-backed (raised from Coinbase Ventures, Binance Labs, others)
notable: Standardizes financial metrics across crypto, often called the Bloomberg terminal for crypto.

name: Tokeny
tagline: ERC-3643 tokenization infrastructure
description: Tokeny is an enterprise tokenization platform and the steward of the ERC-3643 permissioned-token standard, providing issuance, compliance and lifecycle management for tokenized securities. Apex Group is a majority owner, embedding Tokeny in institutional fund administration.
notable: Creator of the widely used ERC-3643 security-token standard.

name: Tokocrypto
tagline: Binance-backed Indonesian crypto exchange
description: Tokocrypto is an Indonesian centralized crypto exchange launched in 2018 and backed by Binance. It offers 400+ tokens with spot and Pro trading, IDR on/off-ramps and an Earn yield feature, operating under Indonesian (Bappebti/OJK) regulation.
notable: Binance-backed exchange and a leading Indonesian on-ramp.

name: Toku
tagline: Stablecoin payroll and token compensation for global teams
description: Toku is an all-in-one global payroll, EOR and token-compensation platform purpose-built for crypto and Web3 companies. It handles fiat employment, stablecoin disbursements and token-grant compliance across 100+ jurisdictions, integrating with ADP, Rippling and Workday and partnering with Mesh for end-to-end stablecoin payroll rails.
notable: Built the first global system for compliant token compensation and stablecoin payroll.

name: TON
tagline: Telegram-linked L1 for in-app stablecoin payments
description: The Open Network (TON) is a Layer-1 closely integrated with Telegram, enabling stablecoin payments to a massive messaging user base. USDT on TON has driven significant peer-to-peer and in-app dollar transfer adoption via Telegram wallets.
notable: Distributes stablecoin payments to Telegram's user base via in-app wallets.

name: Tonkeeper
tagline: Leading non-custodial wallet for TON
description: Tonkeeper is the leading self-custody wallet for The Open Network (TON), the chain tied to Telegram. It supports USDT on TON (a major stablecoin venue) plus staking and dApps, with deep Telegram integration.
notable: Primary wallet for TON, a major USDT venue via Telegram.

name: Topper (Uphold)
tagline: Uphold's embeddable fiat-to-crypto onramp
description: Topper is Uphold's on/off-ramp product, an embeddable fiat-to-crypto checkout backed by Uphold's regulated exchange infrastructure, supporting cards, bank transfers, and many assets globally.
notable: Ramp arm of regulated exchange Uphold.

name: Toros / dHEDGE
tagline: Automated on-chain asset management and yield vaults
