description: dHEDGE is a decentralized, non-custodial asset management protocol enabling on-chain investment funds, and Toros is its automated-strategy product offering dynamic vaults including leveraged tokens and stablecoin yield vaults (USDy, USDpy delta-neutral). The vaults deliver tokenized yield strategies on top of stablecoin and crypto holdings.
funding: Raised seed funding incl. backing from BlockTower and Maple co-founders
notable: Toros is dHEDGE's automated strategy layer offering tokenized leverage and yield.

name: Totalis
tagline: On-chain derivative layer for prediction markets
description: Totalis is a DeFi startup building a derivative layer for prediction markets, letting users bundle views across multiple events rather than betting on a single outcome. Founded in 2026, it runs fully on-chain using stablecoins for transactions and treasury management, and became the recipient of Y Combinator's first all-stablecoin seed investment, a $500K USDC disbursement settled on Solana.
notable: First company to receive a Y Combinator seed investment paid entirely in stablecoins (USDC on Solana).

name: Trace Finance
tagline: Stablecoin and banking infrastructure for Brazil and LatAm
description: Trace Finance is a regulated financial-infrastructure company powering cross-border payments, banking connectivity, FX and stablecoin settlement across Brazil, the US and emerging markets. It combines local rails (Pix), compliance, banking infrastructure and stablecoin-enabled settlement via one API, enabling BRL-to-stablecoin conversion in under a minute. It has processed over $10B in cross-border volume and serves major PSPs including dLocal.
notable: Series A backed by CoinFund, Coinbase Ventures, Haun Ventures, Jump Crypto, Valor Capital and Paxos; valuation grew 10x from seed.

name: Tradable
tagline: Private credit tokenization and liquidity platform
description: Tradable is a private credit tokenization and liquidity platform offering deal ownership management and access to institutional-grade private credit deals onchain. It operates on the ZKsync Era network and has tokenized over $2 billion in real-world private credit assets, making private credit positions transferable and composable in DeFi.
notable: Over $2B in tokenized private credit assets on ZKsync Era.

name: Trail of Bits
tagline: Security research and smart contract audits
description: Trail of Bits is a cybersecurity research and consulting firm known for high-assurance smart contract audits, cryptography review, and security tooling such as Slither and Echidna. It audits stablecoin protocols, bridges, and DeFi systems for correctness and exploit resistance.
notable: Author of widely used open-source smart contract analysis tools Slither and Echidna.

name: Transak
tagline: Global fiat-to-crypto on/off-ramp developer infrastructure
description: Transak provides an embeddable on/off-ramp widget, SDK, and API supporting 130+ cryptocurrencies and local payment methods across 60+ countries. It is widely integrated into wallets, dApps, NFT marketplaces, games, and exchanges, and partnered with Cross River Bank in 2025 for ACH, wire, RTP, and FedNow rails.
notable: Partnered with Cross River Bank in 2025 for US bank rails.

name: Transfero
tagline: Issuer of BRZ, the Brazilian real stablecoin
description: Transfero is a Brazilian financial group that issues BRZ, the leading Brazilian-real-pegged stablecoin, used for on/off-ramps, remittances and trading across Latin America and multiple blockchains.
notable: Leading Brazilian real stablecoin.

name: TransFi
tagline: Stablecoin and cross-border payment solutions for global businesses
description: TransFi provides global on/off-ramps and stablecoin payout/collection rails supporting 250+ payment methods across 100+ countries, used by businesses for cross-border collections and disbursements.
notable: Focus on emerging-market cross-border corridors across Asia, Africa and LatAm.

name: Trezor
tagline: Pioneer hardware wallet maker
description: Trezor, built by SatoshiLabs, created the first commercial hardware wallet. Its devices (Model One, Model T, Safe series) and Trezor Suite app provide cold-storage self-custody for Bitcoin, Ethereum and stablecoins like USDC and USDT.
notable: Built the first commercial hardware wallet.

name: Tria
tagline: Chain-abstracted Web3 neobank wallet
description: Tria is a self-custodial Web3 neobank and chain-abstraction platform that lets users trade, spend and earn across EVM, Solana, Cosmos, Move and Bitcoin ecosystems without bridges, gas tokens or network switching. It offers a consumer wallet plus an embedded wallet SDK for developers, stablecoin yield on idle assets, and a BestPath intent-routing layer that finds optimal cross-chain execution.
notable: Chain abstraction across EVM, Solana, Cosmos, Move and Bitcoin.

name: Triple-A
tagline: Regulated stablecoin payment and payout gateway
description: Triple-A is a regulated crypto/stablecoin payment gateway offering local-currency settlement with fixed FX rates and fast bank payouts across the US, Europe and Asia. It works directly with major banks (DBS, Standard Chartered, BNY Mellon, OCBC, Deutsche Bank) and supports stablecoin acceptance and disbursement.
notable: Regulated in US, Europe and Asia; settles via tier-1 banks.

name: TRM Labs
tagline: Blockchain intelligence for risk and compliance
description: TRM Labs provides blockchain analytics, transaction monitoring, wallet screening and forensic investigation tools focused on sanctions compliance and national security. It achieved FedRAMP High authorization and operates the Beacon Network for real-time intelligence sharing. TRM reached a $1B valuation in its 2026 Series C.
notable: FedRAMP High authorized; strong sanctions/national-security focus.

name: TRON
tagline: Dominant network for USDT settlement
description: TRON is a high-throughput, low-fee Layer-1 founded by Justin Sun that has become the dominant settlement network for USDT, hosting roughly 60%+ of total USDT supply and the majority of USDT transfers by count and volume. It is heavily used for remittances and peer-to-peer dollar transfers in emerging markets.
notable: Hosts the majority of circulating USDT; primary remittance rail in emerging markets.

name: Tron-rival: Aptos
tagline: High-throughput Move L1 with growing stablecoin supply
description: Aptos is a high-throughput Layer-1 using the Move language, which has attracted significant native stablecoin supply including USDC, USDT, and Ethena's USDe. It positions itself for fast, low-cost payments and institutional stablecoin and RWA use cases.
notable: Move-based L1 with rapidly growing native stablecoin liquidity.

name: Trubit
tagline: LatAm exchange and cross-border stablecoin payments
description: Trubit is a Latin American crypto exchange and payments platform offering on/off-ramps and stablecoin-based cross-border transfers, with a B2B arm serving businesses across Mexico and the region.
notable: Active in Mexican cross-border and stablecoin payment corridors.

name: TrueFi
tagline: On-chain credit and RWA lending protocol
description: TrueFi is a DeFi credit protocol, originally built by the TrustToken team, that pioneered uncollateralized on-chain lending with on-chain credit scores. After originating over $1.5B in undercollateralized loans during the prior cycle, it pivoted toward real-world-asset lending and tokenized credit. The protocol is governed by the TRU token.
funding: Backed by Andreessen Horowitz, BlockTower, Alameda (early)
notable: One of the first protocols to bring uncollateralized lending and on-chain credit scoring to DeFi.

name: TrueUSD
tagline: Issuer of TUSD
description: TrueUSD (TUSD) is a USD-pegged stablecoin with on-chain attestations of reserves, live across many public chains. The TrueUSD brand has also issued other fiat-pegged tokens such as TrueGBP, TrueAUD and TrueCAD.
notable: Early stablecoin with live on-chain reserve attestations.

name: Truflation
tagline: On-chain inflation and economic data oracle
description: Truflation provides independent, real-time inflation and macroeconomic data on-chain, serving as a specialized data oracle for RWA, yield, and stablecoin protocols that need real-world economic indices. It aggregates millions of price points to publish daily inflation indexes across regions.
notable: Leading on-chain inflation/macro data oracle for RWA and yield products.

name: Trust Wallet
tagline: Multichain mobile self-custody wallet
description: Trust Wallet is a popular mobile-first self-custody wallet (acquired by Binance in 2018, now operating independently) supporting 100+ blockchains and millions of assets. It supports USDC anywhere Circle issues natively plus bridged stablecoin variants on 70+ EVM chains.
notable: One of the largest mobile crypto wallets globally.

name: Turnkey
tagline: Non-custodial key infrastructure and embedded wallets
description: Turnkey is non-custodial wallet and key-management infrastructure spanning embedded wallets and company wallets. Keys are generated, stored, and used for signing entirely inside hardware-isolated secure enclaves, with an independently verifiable security model. Popular for high-throughput signing and agent/stablecoin applications.
notable: Enclave-based signing infrastructure popular with fintechs and agents.

name: U.S. Bank
tagline: Custodian for reserves backing Anchorage Digital's stablecoins
description: U.S. Bancorp's U.S. Bank is one of the largest commercial banks in the United States and was selected to provide custody services for the reserves backing payment stablecoins issued by Anchorage Digital Bank, including USAT.
notable: Custodies reserves backing Anchorage Digital Bank's payment stablecoins.

name: UBS
tagline: Global bank tokenizing funds and money markets
description: UBS is a global Swiss bank and wealth manager active in tokenization, having launched tokenized money-market fund (uMINT) and blockchain-based payment pilots. It provides banking, asset management, and reserve-grade services relevant to stablecoins and yield-bearing dollar products.
notable: Launched uMINT, a tokenized money-market fund, and ran cross-border payment pilots.

name: UMA
tagline: Optimistic oracle for arbitrary data
description: UMA is an optimistic oracle that verifies arbitrary off-chain data and claims through a propose-and-dispute mechanism resolved by token-holder voting. It underpins prediction markets (e.g. Polymarket), insurance, and custom data resolution rather than high-frequency price feeds.
notable: Optimistic Oracle powers Polymarket and arbitrary on-chain verification.

name: Umbrella Network
tagline: Scalable Merkle-tree-batched oracle
description: Umbrella Network is a decentralized oracle that batches large volumes of data into Merkle trees to deliver many price feeds cost-efficiently on-chain. It targets high-volume DeFi data needs with low per-feed gas costs across multiple EVM chains.
notable: Merkle-batched feeds for low-cost high-volume data delivery.

name: Unbound Security
tagline: Cryptographic MPC security (acquired by Coinbase)
description: Unbound Security was an Israel-based pioneer in cryptographic key-management and secure multi-party computation (MPC). Coinbase acquired Unbound Security in late 2021 to bolster its institutional custody, wallet and key-management technology. (Note: Unbound was acquired by Coinbase, not PayPal.)
notable: Acquired by Coinbase to strengthen its MPC and key-management stack.

name: Unichain
tagline: DeFi-focused L2 by Uniswap Labs
description: Unichain is an Ethereum Layer 2 built on the OP Stack and launched by Uniswap Labs, optimized for fast, low-cost DeFi and cross-chain liquidity. It serves as a home base for Uniswap trading and the broader DeFi stack, with deep stablecoin liquidity in USDC, USDT and DAI.
funding: Developed by Uniswap Labs (backed by a16z, Paradigm)
notable: Uniswap Labs' own Layer 2, built to capture DeFi liquidity in the Superchain.

name: Union
tagline: Zero-knowledge interoperability layer
description: Union is a zero-knowledge interoperability protocol providing trust-minimized cross-chain messaging and asset transfers using ZK light clients, connecting Cosmos, Ethereum, and other ecosystems. It targets censorship-resistant bridging for stablecoins and tokens.
notable: ZK light-client based trust-minimized interoperability.

name: Uniswap
tagline: Largest decentralized exchange
description: Uniswap is the largest DEX by volume, enabling permissionless token swaps via concentrated-liquidity AMMs. Stablecoin pairs (USDC/ETH, USDC/USDT) are among its highest-volume markets, making it a core stablecoin trading and liquidity venue. Uniswap v4 introduced hooks for customizable pools, and the protocol launched its own Unichain L2.
notable: Highest-volume DEX; stablecoin pairs are top markets.

name: Unit21
tagline: No-code fraud & AML operations platform
description: Unit21 is a risk and compliance platform offering no-code and agentic AI tools for fraud prevention, AML transaction monitoring, case management and regulatory reporting. Trusted by 200+ fintechs, banks and crypto firms, it helps stablecoin and payments companies monitor transactions and file suspicious activity reports.
funding: $100M+ raised (Series C led by Tiger Global, ICONIQ)
notable: No-code AML/fraud platform used by Chime, Intuit and major fintechs.

name: United Stables
tagline: Issuer of $U, the stablecoin liquidity layer
description: United Stables issues $U, a USD-pegged stablecoin deployed natively on BNB Chain and Ethereum, designed as a liquidity layer to reduce fragmentation of stablecoin liquidity across networks. It rose into the top stablecoins shortly after launch.
notable: Fast-rising stablecoin native to BNB Chain.

name: Unlimit (Unlimit Crypto)
tagline: Global payments and fiat<>crypto on/off-ramp
description: Unlimit is a global payments company whose Unlimit Crypto product offers fiat<>crypto on/off-ramps with 1000+ payment methods and broad geographic licensing, enabling Web3 platforms to embed buy/sell flows worldwide.
notable: Large traditional PSP with a dedicated crypto ramp arm.

name: Upbit
tagline: Dominant South Korean exchange
description: Upbit, operated by Dunamu, is the dominant crypto exchange in South Korea by volume. While much Korean trading is in KRW, Upbit is a major regional venue for USDT and stablecoin-related products as Korea moves toward won-stablecoin regulation.
notable: Largest South Korean exchange by volume.

name: Usual
tagline: Issuer of USD0 RWA-backed stablecoin and USD0++ bond
description: Usual is a decentralized fiat stablecoin protocol whose USD0 is backed by tokenized short-term US Treasuries. USD0++ is a yield-bearing 'liquid bond' with fixed maturity and protocol token (USUAL) rewards. Led by former French politician Pierre Person, Usual positions itself as a community-owned challenger to Tether.
funding: ~$17M+ (Kraken Ventures, IOSG, Binance Labs, GSR, Mantle)
notable: Distributes protocol ownership to stablecoin users via the USUAL token.

name: Utila
tagline: Operational MPC wallet platform for businesses
description: Utila is an institutional MPC wallet and digital asset operations platform aimed at fintechs, payment firms and funds, with high-throughput stablecoin operations, policy controls and treasury tooling. It has grown rapidly as a custody/operations layer for stablecoin money movement.
notable: Fast-growing MPC operations layer for stablecoin payments.

name: VALR
tagline: Leading African crypto exchange
description: VALR is a leading South African crypto exchange expanding across Africa and beyond. It supports broad stablecoin trading and payments, positioning itself as a key venue in a region with strong stablecoin remittance and savings use cases.
notable: One of Africa's largest exchanges by volume.

name: VanEck
tagline: Tokenized Treasury fund (VBILL)
description: VanEck, a global asset manager, issues VBILL, a tokenized US Treasury fund via Securitize. The fund is integrated into DeFi (e.g. usable as collateral on Euler) as VanEck pushes tokenized funds into on-chain markets.
notable: VBILL went live as DeFi collateral on Euler in 2026.

name: Velodrome
tagline: ve(3,3) liquidity layer DEX on Optimism / Superchain
description: Velodrome is the leading ve(3,3) automated market maker and liquidity hub on Optimism and the broader Superchain. It uses vote-escrowed VELO (veVELO) to direct emissions toward pools, making it the central venue for incentivizing stablecoin and bluechip liquidity on OP-stack chains. Its sister deployment on Base is Aerodrome.
notable: The dominant DEX and emissions market on Optimism; sister protocol to Aerodrome on Base.

name: Venus Protocol
tagline: Money market and synthetic stablecoin (VAI) on BNB Chain
description: Venus Protocol is one of the largest lending and borrowing money markets, originating on BNB Chain and now multichain. Beyond supplying and borrowing assets including USDC, USDT and FDUSD, Venus issues VAI, an overcollateralized decentralized stablecoin minted against deposited collateral. It operates isolated pools and a core pool governed by the XVS token.
notable: Issuer of the VAI decentralized stablecoin; a leading BNB Chain lending protocol.

name: Veriff
tagline: AI-powered identity verification
description: Veriff is an AI-powered identity verification provider offering document, biometric and proof-of-address checks plus fraud detection, used by crypto exchanges, fintechs and marketplaces for KYC onboarding.
notable: Estonian IDV unicorn popular with crypto exchanges.

name: VerifyVASP
tagline: Verified VASP Travel Rule network
description: VerifyVASP operates a closed, centralized Travel Rule messaging network for secure data sharing between verified VASPs, with deep Asia-Pacific coverage. It acquired Japan-based Sygna to expand its Verified Network.
notable: Acquired Sygna; strong APAC VASP coverage.

name: Verto
tagline: B2B cross-border payments and FX for emerging markets
description: Verto is a B2B cross-border payments and FX platform connecting businesses to hard-to-reach emerging-market currencies, using stablecoin rails alongside local infrastructure to speed settlement and reduce costs across Africa and beyond.
funding: $10M Series A (2021), backed by Quona, MEVP, Treasury
notable: Y Combinator alum focused on emerging-market B2B FX.

name: Visa
tagline: Card network settling and enabling stablecoins
description: Visa is a global card network that has integrated stablecoins for settlement and card programs. Visa settles transactions in USDC, partnered with Bridge (Stripe) to let fintechs issue Visa cards funded by stablecoins with on-the-fly off-ramp conversion, and invested in BVNK. Its stablecoin settlement volume hit a ~$4.5B annualized run rate by early 2026.
notable: Pioneered USDC settlement among card networks; partnered with Bridge and invested in BVNK.

name: Visa Direct
tagline: Visa's global money-movement and payouts network
description: Visa Direct is Visa's real-time push-payments and payouts platform reaching billions of endpoints across cards, accounts, and wallets in 190+ countries. In 2025 Visa launched stablecoin pilots on Visa Direct: a prefunding pilot letting businesses fund payout accounts with USDC or PYUSD, and a payouts pilot enabling recipients to receive funds directly in stablecoin wallets, with wider rollout planned for 2026.
notable: 2025 pilots let businesses prefund with USDC/PYUSD and pay recipients directly into stablecoin wallets.

name: VNX
tagline: Non-USD stablecoins and tokenized gold from Liechtenstein
description: VNX is a Liechtenstein-based issuer of regulated non-USD stablecoins and tokenized real-world assets, operating under the Liechtenstein Blockchain Act and licensed by the Financial Market Authority (FMA). Its products include VEUR (euro), VCHF (Swiss franc), VGBP (British pound) and VNXAU (tokenized gold), each fully backed 1:1 by reserves held in Swiss and Liechtenstein banks. VNX stablecoins are integrated with exchanges and institutions including Bitstamp, SwissBorg, Sygnum Bank and Wirex.
notable: Regulatory-grade issuance layer for non-USD stablecoins and tokenized precious metals under the Liechtenstein Blockchain Act.

name: Wanchain
tagline: Decentralized interoperability blockchain
description: Wanchain is a long-running interoperability-focused Layer 1 that provides decentralized cross-chain bridges using secure multiparty computation and Shnorr-threshold signatures, connecting Bitcoin, Ethereum, Cosmos, Polkadot, and EVM chains for asset and stablecoin transfers.
notable: One of the earliest interoperability-focused chains (2017).

name: WazirX
tagline: Major Indian crypto exchange
description: WazirX is one of India's largest crypto exchanges. It is a key venue in the Indian market for stablecoin and crypto trading, though it suffered a major security breach in 2024 and has been restructuring.
notable: Suffered a ~$230M hack in 2024 and entered restructuring.

name: Web3Auth
tagline: Social-login MPC wallet infra (now MetaMask Embedded Wallets)
description: Web3Auth is pluggable embedded-wallet infrastructure providing social/OAuth login and non-custodial MPC wallets with SDKs for React, Android, Unity and more. It was acquired by MetaMask/Consensys and rebranded MetaMask Embedded Wallets.
notable: Acquired by MetaMask; rebranded as MetaMask Embedded Wallets.

name: Wellington Management
tagline: Institutional asset manager behind tokenized treasury strategies
description: Wellington Management is a major independent asset manager (~$1.2T AUM) that sub-manages the Delta Wellington Ultra Short Treasury On-Chain Fund (ULTRA), a tokenized US Treasury strategy issued by Libeara/FundBridge and distributed via DigiFT. It is also an institutional backer of Ondo Finance and a participant in Citi-led private-market tokenization pilots.
keyProducts: Delta Wellington Ultra Short Treasury On-Chain Fund (ULTRA)
notable: Sub-manager of the tokenized ULTRA treasury fund; Ondo backer.

name: Wells Fargo
tagline: Major US bank with digital-payments initiatives
description: Wells Fargo is a top-four US bank that has explored bank-led stablecoin and tokenized-deposit initiatives, including participation in joint bank stablecoin discussions. It provides banking, payments, and treasury services relevant to stablecoin reserves and settlement.
notable: Previously piloted an internal settlement token (Wells Fargo Digital Cash).

name: Wert
tagline: Embeddable fiat onramp for NFTs and crypto
description: Wert provides an embeddable fiat-to-crypto onramp and smart-contract checkout enabling users to buy crypto, NFTs, and tokens with cards directly inside dApps, with strong support for on-chain minting flows.
notable: Pioneer of fiat-to-smart-contract checkout.

name: Western Alliance Bank
tagline: US bank with blockchain payments and digital-asset banking
description: Western Alliance Bank is a US commercial bank with a dedicated blockchain and digital-assets practice, providing banking and reserve services to digital-asset firms and operating tokenized-deposit payment rails via the Tassat/TassatPay network.
notable: Early bank adopter of blockchain-based tokenized-deposit payments via Tassat.

name: Western Union
tagline: Issuer of USDPT
description: Western Union launched USDPT, a USD stablecoin on Solana issued via Anchorage Digital Bank, to settle global transactions and power its Digital Asset Network — a major legacy remittance player entering stablecoin issuance.
notable: Legacy remittance giant launching its own stablecoin.

name: WhiteBIT
tagline: Large European exchange
description: WhiteBIT is a large European crypto exchange, the biggest in Europe by some user metrics, with a strong presence in Eastern Europe. It supports broad stablecoin trading across USDT, USDC and EUR-pegged tokens.
notable: One of the largest European crypto exchanges.

name: Wirex
tagline: Crypto and stablecoin Visa card
description: Wirex is a long-running crypto payments app offering a custodial Visa debit card with instant conversion, multi-currency spending across 30+ assets and tiered WXT Cryptoback rewards, plus a stablecoin-focused card offering.
notable: One of the earliest crypto debit cards; now stablecoin-focused.

name: WisdomTree
tagline: Asset manager adapting tokenized funds for stablecoin reserves
description: WisdomTree, a public asset manager, runs WisdomTree Connect and WisdomTree Prime, offering a suite of 13+ SEC-registered tokenized funds across asset classes. It received SEC approval for 24/7 trading and instant USDC settlement of its tokenized MMF (WTGXX).
notable: Revamping tokenized funds to serve as stablecoin reserves.

name: Wise
tagline: International money transfers and multi-currency accounts
description: Wise (formerly TransferWise) is a publicly listed cross-border payments company offering low-cost international transfers, multi-currency accounts, and the Wise Platform infrastructure used by banks and fintechs. It connects directly to domestic payment systems globally and has explored stablecoin and tokenized-money rails as the cross-border landscape evolves.
funding: LSE IPO 2021 (later moved primary listing to US)
notable: One of the largest independent cross-border consumer payments networks, connecting directly into domestic clearing systems.

name: Witnet
tagline: Decentralized oracle blockchain
description: Witnet is a decentralized oracle network with its own blockchain that retrieves, attests, and delivers off-chain data to smart contracts using a layer-1 consensus and randomly assigned witnessing nodes. It provides price feeds and arbitrary web data to multiple EVM chains.
notable: Has its own L1 dedicated to decentralized oracle witnessing.

name: World Chain
tagline: Human-centric L2 for the World network
description: World Chain is an Ethereum Layer 2 built on the OP Stack as part of the World (formerly Worldcoin) project co-founded by Sam Altman and Tools for Humanity. It prioritizes verified human users via World ID, gives priority blockspace to humans, and is designed for global payments and stablecoin use, with USDC available as a primary settlement asset.
funding: Tools for Humanity backed by a16z, Khosla Ventures and others
notable: Part of the Worldcoin/World proof-of-human network co-founded by Sam Altman.

name: World Liberty Financial
tagline: Issuer of USD1
description: World Liberty Financial, a Trump-family-linked venture, issues USD1, a USD stablecoin backed by cash and short-duration US Treasuries and custodied by BitGo. Launched in 2025, USD1 became one of the fastest-growing fiat-backed stablecoins and is deployed across many chains including Ethereum, BNB Chain and Tempo.
notable: Fast-growing politically-linked USD stablecoin.

name: Worldline
tagline: European payment processing and acquiring giant
description: Worldline is a publicly listed European payments company providing merchant acquiring, payment processing, and financial-services technology across dozens of countries. As one of Europe's largest processors it is positioned among incumbent PSPs evaluating stablecoin and digital-currency settlement, including work tied to the digital euro and tokenized payments.
notable: One of the largest payment processors in Europe.

name: Worldpay
tagline: Global acquirer offering USDC settlement and stablecoin payouts
description: Worldpay is one of the world's largest merchant acquirers/processors. It has offered merchant settlement in USDC since 2022 and partnered with BVNK in 2025 to enable near-instant stablecoin payouts to recipients across 180+ markets. Worldpay is being acquired by Global Payments in a deal expected to close in 2026.
funding: Owned by GTCR / FIS; ~$22.7B Global Payments deal
notable: Early mover on USDC merchant settlement; partnered with BVNK for global stablecoin payouts.

name: Wormhole
tagline: Cross-chain messaging and token transfer protocol
description: Wormhole is a generic cross-chain messaging protocol secured by a 19-member Guardian set with staking-based economic security. It offers Native Token Transfers (NTT) for burning/minting tokens across chains without liquidity pools and a CCTP bridge, and powers cross-chain transfers for tokenized assets like BlackRock's BUIDL and Ethena's USDe.
funding: $225M (2023, Brevan Howard, Coinbase Ventures, Multicoin); W token launched 2024
notable: NTT adopted by Ethena USDe and Lido wstETH; powers BUIDL cross-chain transfers.

name: WrappedCBDC
tagline: Issuer of cNGN, Nigeria's first regulated naira stablecoin
description: WrappedCBDC is the licensed Nigerian entity, composed of Convexity Technologies, Interstellar and AlphaGeek and operating via the African Stablecoin Consortium, that issues cNGN (compliant Naira). Pegged 1:1 to the Nigerian naira and launched in February 2025 under Nigerian SEC oversight, cNGN is backed by onshore bank deposits and short-term securities and is positioned as Africa's first regulated stablecoin.
notable: Issuer of Africa's first regulated naira-backed stablecoin, cNGN.

name: Wyoming Stable Token Commission
tagline: Issuer of FRNT (Frontier Stable Token)
description: The Wyoming Stable Token Commission issues FRNT, the first fully-reserved stablecoin issued by a US public entity, backed exclusively by US dollars and short-term Treasuries managed by Franklin Templeton, with surplus income earmarked for public schools.
notable: First US state-issued stablecoin.

name: XREX
tagline: Stablecoin-powered cross-border payments and exchange for SMEs
description: XREX is a Taipei-founded blockchain financial institution providing cross-border B2B payments, fiat-crypto conversion, exchange, custody and on/off-ramps for emerging-market SMEs. Its BitCheck escrow tool, built into XREX Pay, lets businesses settle in fiat (USD, SGD) and stablecoins (USDT, USDC) in minutes. XREX is a regulated VASP in Taiwan and holds an MAS Major Payment Institution licence in Singapore.
notable: Stablecoin-based escrow cross-border payments for emerging-market SME trade.

name: XRP Ledger
tagline: Payments-focused L1 and home of RLUSD
description: The XRP Ledger (XRPL) is a payments-optimized Layer-1 maintained by the Ripple ecosystem, offering fast, low-cost settlement and built-in DEX features. It is the issuance home of Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin and is supported as a settlement network by Mastercard, positioning it for institutional cross-border and stablecoin payments.
notable: Native home of RLUSD; supported by Mastercard for stablecoin settlement.

name: XTransfer
tagline: B2B cross-border payments for SMEs, exploring stablecoin rails
description: XTransfer is a B2B cross-border financial and payments platform founded in 2017, often described as a 'B2B Alipay'. It serves over 800,000 enterprises and processes more than $12B in payments monthly, covering over 2% of China's exports. Its CEO Bill Deng argues stablecoins' biggest application is cross-border B2B trade, positioning the firm to use stablecoin rails to cut intermediary-bank fees and settlement times.
notable: Processes over 2% of China's exports; Global South flows account for ~70% of volume.

name: Yala
tagline: Bitcoin-backed stablecoin YU
description: Yala is a Bitcoin-native stablecoin protocol that lets BTC holders mint YU, an overcollateralized stablecoin backed by Bitcoin, enabling DeFi liquidity from BTC across multiple chains. It is a notable 2024-2025 entrant in the Bitcoin DeFi (BTCFi) space.
notable: Bitcoin-collateralized stablecoin (BTCFi).

name: Yearn Finance
tagline: DeFi yield aggregator / vaults
description: Yearn is a pioneering yield aggregator whose vaults auto-allocate deposited assets (including stablecoins like USDC, DAI and crvUSD) across DeFi strategies to optimize returns. Its V3 vault architecture and yCRV/yETH products remain widely used building blocks for automated stablecoin yield.
notable: Original DeFi vault aggregator; fair-launched YFI.

name: Yei Finance
tagline: Leading money market on Sei
description: Yei Finance is the leading lending/borrowing money market on the Sei network, providing overcollateralized loans, flash loans, and yields on assets including USDT and USDC. It is building a cross-chain money market (Clovis architecture) that allocates single deposits across networks, and includes YeiSwap and YeiBridge.
notable: Largest DeFi protocol on Sei by TVL.

name: Yellow Card
tagline: Pan-African stablecoin payments and on/off-ramps
description: Yellow Card is a licensed stablecoin payments platform operating in 20+ African countries, providing cross-border B2B payments, on/off-ramps and treasury via stablecoins. It partners with Visa and is a Circle Payments Network design partner.
notable: Partnered with Visa in 2025 to explore stablecoin use cases across Africa.

name: Yield Basis
tagline: Bitcoin yield protocol from Curve's founder
description: Yield Basis is a DeFi protocol launched in 2025 by Curve Finance founder Michael Egorov that generates sustainable Bitcoin yield while eliminating impermanent loss. Users deposit BTC to mint a ybBTC receipt token; the protocol borrows crvUSD to pair the BTC in a Curve pool at 2x leverage, using the LP position as collateral. It leans on Curve infrastructure, uses crvUSD as a core stablecoin component, and rewards veYB lockers with protocol fees paid in crvUSD or wrapped BTC.
notable: First project to debut on the joint Legion and Kraken launchpad; built by Curve founder Michael Egorov.

name: Zand Bank
tagline: UAE digital bank issuing the first regulated AED stablecoin
description: Zand Bank is a UAE AI-powered digital bank (rated BBB+ by Fitch) that launched Zand AED, the country's first regulated, multi-chain AED-backed stablecoin on public blockchains, approved by the Central Bank of the UAE. Issued via subsidiary Zand Trust and built with Taurus tokenization and wallet technology, Zand AED is fully backed 1:1 by dirham reserves in segregated accounts with independently audited smart contracts and reserve attestations.
notable: Issued the UAE's first regulated AED-backed stablecoin on public blockchains.

name: Zelle (Early Warning Services)
tagline: US bank-owned real-time P2P payments network
description: Zelle is a US peer-to-peer and account-to-account payments network operated by Early Warning Services, a consortium owned by seven major US banks including JPMorgan, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo. It moves hundreds of billions of dollars annually directly between bank accounts and is exploring stablecoin and international expansion as bank-led alternatives to card and crypto rails develop.
funding: Owned by Early Warning Services (7-bank consortium)
notable: Bank-owned network whose owners have reportedly explored a joint bank stablecoin.

name: Zengo
tagline: Keyless MPC consumer wallet
description: Zengo is a consumer self-custody wallet using MPC to eliminate seed phrases, with biometric and 3D-face recovery. It supports Bitcoin, Ethereum and stablecoins like USDC and USDT and markets itself on being unhacked since launch.
notable: Seedless MPC consumer wallet emphasizing security.

name: Zepz
tagline: Digital remittances via WorldRemit and Sendwave
description: Zepz is the parent of WorldRemit and Sendwave, major digital remittance brands serving 150+ countries. It is adopting stablecoin settlement (via Fireblocks) to enable near-instant, lower-cost cross-border payments into emerging markets.
funding: $292M Series F (2021), backed by Accel, TCV, Leapfrog
notable: Partnered with Fireblocks in 2025 to scale stablecoin remittances; Circle Payments Network design partner.

name: Zerion
tagline: Self-custody wallet with DeFi portfolio management
description: Zerion is a self-custody wallet and DeFi portfolio manager supporting multiple chains, thousands of ERC-20 tokens, liquidity pools and vaults, with tools to earn interest on stablecoins. Available in Ledger Live and across mobile and extension.
notable: Combines wallet with strong portfolio analytics.

name: Zero Hash
tagline: Embedded stablecoin & crypto infrastructure APIs
description: Zero Hash is regulated B2B2C crypto and stablecoin infrastructure that abstracts blockchain complexity behind simple APIs/SDKs, powering stablecoin payments, on/off-ramps, payouts and settlement for PSPs and fintechs. It reached unicorn status with a $104M Series D-2 in Sept 2025 (Interactive Brokers, Morgan Stanley, Apollo, SoFi), bringing total funding to $275M.
notable: Unicorn embedded stablecoin/crypto infra for PSPs.

name: zkSync
tagline: ZK-powered Elastic Network by Matter Labs
description: zkSync, built by Matter Labs, is a zero-knowledge Layer-2 scaling solution for Ethereum that evolved into the Elastic Network, an interconnected system of autonomous, interoperable ZK chains sharing liquidity and security. The ZK token launched in June 2024 with a 21B max supply and a just-in-time minting model. The ZK Stack lets chains use ERC20 tokens such as USDC as the base gas token, and its Prividium offering powers institutional and tokenized-deposit networks with partners including Deutsche Bank, UBS and Sygnum. The network has secured over $4B in value.
notable: Pioneered the Elastic Network of ZK chains; Prividium powers tokenized bank-deposit networks.

name: Zodia Custody
tagline: Bank-backed institutional custody (Standard Chartered)
description: Zodia Custody is an institutional digital asset custodian majority-owned by Standard Chartered with SBI and Northern Trust as investors. FCA-registered in the UK and authorized by Luxembourg's CSSF, it offers a MiCA-compatible custody route for EU-domiciled funds. Standard Chartered moved to fully acquire Zodia in 2026.
funding: Backed by Standard Chartered, SBI, Northern Trust
notable: Majority-owned by Standard Chartered; MiCA-ready.

name: Zoth
tagline: RWA restaking and yield protocol
description: Zoth is an RWA protocol that lets institutions deposit tokenized real-world assets such as US Treasuries and corporate credit and restake them in DeFi, earning both off-chain yield and on-chain yield. Its ZeUSD stablecoin is collateralized by yield-bearing RWAs, and the platform targets MiCA and Basel III alignment.
notable: Promotes a dual-yield model where tokenized Treasuries earn government interest and on-chain DeFi yield simultaneously.
